Ayah

ٱقْتَرَبَ لِلنَّاسِ حِسَابُهُمْ وَهُمْ فِى غَفْلَةٍ مُّعْرِضُونَ (۱)
Mankind’s reckoning has drawn near to them, yet they are disregardful in [their] obliviousness. (1)

Key Points

| Question: Considering that the meaning of ghafleh (غفلت) is inattention to something, and the meaning of i'radh (اِعراض) is turning away from something with attention, how is it that ghafleh and i'radh are mentioned side-by-side in the Ayah? Answer: Ghafleh (inattention) is of two types: A: Ghafleh from which a person awakens upon warning. B: Ghafleh which is a prelude to i'radh, meaning the heedless person does not want to wake up. This is like someone who pretends to be asleep and does not open his eyes even when called. Since most people are heedless of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the Reckoning, Allah has attributed this heedlessness to all people, otherwise, a group of people is always an exception. Ayah 97 of this very Surah also refers to the people's own admission of heedlessness: «یا ویلنا قد كنا فى غفلة من هذا» A look at the issue of the Reckoning on the Day of Judgment reveals the following from the Ayahs: 1- All individuals will be questioned. «فلنسئلنّ الّذین ارسل الیهم ولنسئلنّ المرسلین»(2) 2- All deeds will be subjected to calculation. «لتُسئلنّ عما كنتم تعملون»(3) 3- All inner states will be examined. «اِن تبدوا ما فى انفسكم او تخفوه یحاسبكم به اللّه»(4) 4- All parts of the human body will be questioned. «اِنّ السّمع و البصر و... كلّ اولئك كان عنه مسئولا»(5) 5 - Questions will be asked about divine blessings. «و لتسئلنّ یومئذٍ عن النعیم»(6) 6- All deeds, wherever they may be and whatever their measure, will be brought forth. «ان تك مثقال حبةّ من خردل فتكن فى صخرة»(7) In the first question, they will ask about the prayer (Salat), and they will address the reckoning of important matters such as youth, lifespan, the means of earning income and spending it, and especially the issue of leadership and Wilayah (guardianship). And it is sufficient that Allah Himself will be the Reckoner. «كفى بناحاسبین»(8)

2) A'raf, 16. 3) Nahl, 93. 4) Baqarah, 284. 5) Isra, 36. 6) Nahl, 93. 7) Luqman, 16. 8) Anbiya, 47.

Messages

1- The reckoning of people is certain. «اقترب» (The use of the past tense verb instead of the present tense indicates certainty.)

2- Let us not consider the Day of Judgment and the time of reckoning to be far off. «اقترب»

3- The reckoning is for all human beings. «للناس»

4- Order and reckoning are prerequisites for a complete life. «حسابهم» (Man is not a random phenomenon, but a purposeful being based on calculation and reckoning.)

5 - Sometimes, to capture attention, speech must begin forcefully and warningly. The Surah begins with the phrase «اقترب للناس حسابهم».

6- It is the reckoning that approaches mankind, even if they are heedless of it or flee from it. «اقترب للناس حسابهم» and it did not say: «اقترب الناس الى حسابهم»

7- Heedlessness (ghafleh) is dangerous when it is based on indifference (i'radh). «غفلة معرضون»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Anbiya Surah Al-Anbiya is Makki and has one hundred and twelve Ayahs. This Surah refers to the stories of sixteen Prophets who were favored by the Lord. Furthermore, in this Surah, threats and warnings (wa'id and indhar) are used more frequently than promises and glad tidings (wa'd and bisharat). Blessings have been mentioned for the recitation of this Surah, including the easing of the reckoning on the Day of Judgment and shaking hands with the Prophets علیهم السلام, provided that this recitation is done lovingly, «حبّاًلها», and serves as a prelude to faith and action.(1)

1) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

مَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِم مُّحْدَثٍ إِلَّا ٱسْتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ (۲)
There does not come to them any new reminder from their Lord but they listen to it as they play around, (2)

Key Points

The issue of whether the Word of God (Kalam Allah) is eternal (Qadim) or created (Hadith) was among the issues raised during the time of the Abbasid Caliphs and became the subject of much debate and discussion. It seems that raising this discussion was primarily political, intended to distract people from fundamental matters, the essential truths of Islam, and confronting oppressive rulers. It is evident that the content of the Quran originates from the knowledge of Allah and is eternal (Qadim), but its wording (Alfaz), which was revealed to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, is created (Hadith) and new. In any case, such discussions do not solve any problem for individuals or society, and our Imams also avoided them.(9)

9) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Divine reminders are accompanied by continuity and repetition. «ما یأتیهم» (present tense verb)

2- Reminder (Tadhakkur) and nurturing (Tarbiyah) are inseparable. «ذكر من ربّهم»

3- The Quran is the source of remembrance (Dhikr). «ذكر من ربّهم»

4- Novelty (innovation) in the reminder is a value. «ذكر من ربّهم محدث»

5- Listening and understanding are not enough; acceptance and action are necessary. «استمعوه و هم یلعبون»

6- The source of diversion (Lahw) and play (La'b) is heedlessness (Ghaflah) and turning away (I'radh). «فى غفلة معرضون - یلعبون»

7- Life devoid of revelation and Divine remembrance is nothing more than a game. «و هم یلعبون»

Ayah

لَاهِيَةً قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّجْوَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ هَلْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ ۖ أَفَتَأْتُونَ ٱلسِّحْرَ وَأَنتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ (۳)
their hearts set on diversions. The wrongdoers secretly whisper together, [saying], ‘Is this [man] not a human being like yourselves? Will you give in to magic with open eyes?’ (3)

Messages

1- Preoccupation with worthless matters distracts man from paying attention to main issues like the Reckoning and the Resurrection. «و هم فى غفلة... لاهیة قلوبهم»

2- Deviant individuals justify their deviation. «بشر مثلكم افتأتون السحر»

3- Unless the heart becomes heedless of Allah, man does not mock the Divine signs. «یلعبون - لاهیة قلوبهم»

4- The opponents of the prophets conspire in secret and consider miracles to be sorcery and magic. «و اسروا النجوى - افتأتون السحر»

5- By concealing the truths, the disbelievers commit injustice towards themselves and others. «ظلموا»

6- The slander of the oppressors against the prophets is a sign that the path of the prophets is anti-oppression. «الّذین ظلموا... هل هذا الابشرٌ»

7- The lives of the prophets among the people were very simple and ordinary. «بشر مثلكم»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّى يَعْلَمُ ٱلْقَوْلَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٤)
He said, ‘My Lord knows every word [spoken] in the sky and on the earth, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.’ (4)

Messages

1- Prophets rely on Allah against conspiracies and accusations. «قال ربّى یعلم القول»

2- Although the enemies conspire, whisper, and act secretly «و اسرّوا النجوى», Allah knows everything, and the hidden and the manifest are the same to Him. «یعلم القول فى السماء والارض»

3- Attention to the knowledge of Allah causes the abandonment of conspiracies and whisperings. «و اسرّوا النجوى ... ربّى یعلم ...»

Ayah

بَلْ قَالُوٓا۟ أَضْغَـٰثُ أَحْلَـٰمٍۭ بَلِ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ بَلْ هُوَ شَاعِرٌ فَلْيَأْتِنَا بِـَٔايَةٍ كَمَآ أُرْسِلَ ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ (٥)
But they said, ‘[They are] muddled dreams!’ ‘Indeed, he has fabricated it!’ ‘Indeed, he is a poet!’ ‘Let him bring us a sign, like those sent to the former generations.’ (5)

Key Points

The word adghāth (اضغاث) is the plural of dhighth (ضِغث), meaning a bundle or collection, and aḥlām (احلام) is the plural of ḥulm (حُلم), meaning a dream. Adghāth Aḥlām (اضغاث احلام) means a collection of scattered dreams. The disbelievers demanded miracles from the Prophet of Islam, similar to the miracles of Moses and Jesus, but their goal was mere excuse-making. This is because the selection of the type of miracle depends on Divine Wisdom, not the whims of the people.

Messages

1- The contradictory statements of the disbelievers are a sign of their lack of logic. «بل... بل... بل...»

2- The enemy is not satisfied with leveling just one accusation. «بل... بل... بل...» (He strikes from all directions: «اسرّوا النجوى ... هل هذا الا بشر... بل هو شاعر», and continuous propaganda attacks and successive accusations are among the main methods employed by the enemy.)

3- Despite all the accusations the enemy levels, he is internally afflicted by doubt.(10) «بل... بل... بل... فلیأتنا بایه»

4- The polytheists also know that the prerequisite for prophethood is the presentation of a miracle. «فلیأتنا»

5- People demand a tangible miracle. «فلیأتنا بآیة» (10) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayahs

مَآ ءَامَنَتْ قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهَآ ۖ أَفَهُمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٦)
No town that We destroyed before them believed. Will these then have faith [if they are sent signs]? (6)
وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا قَبْلَكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِىٓ إِلَيْهِمْ ۖ فَسْـَٔلُوٓا۟ أَهْلَ ٱلذِّكْرِ إِن كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۷)
We did not send [any apostles] before you except as men to whom We revealed. Ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know. (7)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the disbelievers were saying: «هل هذا الاّ بشرٌ مثلكم»? "Is this man anything but a human being like you?" In this verse, Allah says: Not only this Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, but all previous Prophets were also human beings to whom revelation was sent. Being human does not contradict prophethood. Since the polytheists had a good relationship with the People of the Book, Allah tells them: Ask the People of the Book (Jews and Christians). Asking the People of Remembrance (Ahl al-Dhikr) and referring to experts is a rational principle, and in this verse, Ahl al-Dhikr refers to the scholars of the Jews and Christians. In narrations, it is recommended that whenever you are confused, ask the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet علیهم السلام, as those noble ones are the perfect manifestation (Miṣdāq) of Ahl al-Dhikr. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Neither should the scholar remain silent and conceal his knowledge, nor should the ignorant person remain silent about his ignorance. «لاینبغى للعالم أن یسكت على علمه ولا ینبغى للجاهل أن یسكت على جهله و قد قال اللّه تعالى: فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر...»(11)

11) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr.

Messages

1- It is the Divine tradition that wherever there is disbelief and obstinacy, there will be Divine wrath. «ما آمنت... اهلكناهم» (Faith is the cause of the preservation and survival of human societies.)

2- We should look at history with an eye of admonition. «أفهم یؤمنون»

3- All Prophets were men. «و ما ارسلنا قبلك الاّ رجالاً»

4- Revelation was sent to all Prophets, and their knowledge originates from this source. «نوحى الیهم»

5- Asking questions is a religious recommendation. «فاسئلوا»

6- Questioning must be for the sake of understanding, not for anything else. «فاسئلوا ... اِن كنتم لا تعلمون»

7- Unless we ask those who possess it (the knowledge), we will not attain knowledge. «فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر ...»

8- In matters of belief and religion, we must search and investigate until we reach certainty (knowledge). «فاسئلوا...»

9- The scholar must be accessible so that asking him is possible. «فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر»

10- Being merely knowledgeable is not enough; being Ahl al-Dhikr (People of Remembrance) is valuable. (There are many knowledgeable people who are heedless of Allah.) «فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر»

11- Competence (Ahliyyah) is necessary in every matter; having superficial, imitative, or transient information is not effective. «اهل الذّكر»

12- Guiding the people is obligatory upon the Ahl al-Dhikr; otherwise, the command to ask would be null and futile. «فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر»

Ayah

وَمَا جَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ جَسَدًا لَّا يَأْكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ خَـٰلِدِينَ (۸)
We did not make them bodies that did not eat food, nor were they immortal. (8)

Key Points

One of the secrets of a leader's success is that in matters of life, he should be alongside the people and live similarly to them, so that the people may be guided and refined by observing him and his conduct.

Messages

1- The Prophets have no difference from other people regarding material aspects and human needs. «ما جعناهم... لا یأكلون»

2- Expecting the Prophets, reformers, and educators to be angels is an undue expectation. «ما جعناهم...»

3- Abandoning food and spouse is not the will of Allah or the divine religions. «ما جعناهم... لا یأكلون»

4- Death is universal; even the Prophets are not eternal. «ما كانوا خالدین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ صَدَقْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْوَعْدَ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُمْ وَمَن نَّشَآءُ وَأَهْلَكْنَا ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۹)
Then We fulfilled Our promise to them, and We delivered them and whomever We wished, and We destroyed the transgressors. (9)

Key Points

This verse is intended to console and reassure the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, [stating] that We fulfill Our promises regarding the salvation of the believers.

Messages

1- The promise of victory that Allah gave to the prophets is certain. «ثمّ صدقناهم الوعد»

2- Allah's promise to the prophets is their salvation and the destruction of their opponents. «فانجیناهم... و اهلكنا»

3- Divine punishments are the result of our own actions. «اهلكنا المسرفین»

4- Extravagance (Israf) paves the way for destruction. And destruction is the fate of all extravagant people (the transgressors). «المسرفین»

Ayah

لَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكُمْ كِتَـٰبًا فِيهِ ذِكْرُكُمْ ۖ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۱٠)
Certainly We have sent down to you a Book in which there is an admonition for you. Do you not exercise your reason? (10)

Key Points

The word "ذکرکم" (dhikrukum) either means a means of remembrance (tadhakkur) or a source of honor and fame for you. The word "ذکر" (dhikr) linguistically also means nobility and renown. (12) Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The meaning of the phrase "فیه ذکرکم" (fīhi dhikrukum) is that it is mentioned in the Book that obedience after the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله belongs to the Imam. (13) Meaning, your honor and nobility lie in obeying the Imam after the Prophet.

12) Qamus al-Muhit. 13) Ta'wil al-Ayat, p. 319.

Messages

1- A book and law that leads to growth and awakening is one that originates from Allah. "انزلنا" (Anzalnā - We have sent down) - "فیه ذكركم" (fīhi dhikrukum).

2- The Quran possesses an exalted status and a high position. "كتاباً" (Kitāban). (14)

3- One of the goals of sending down heavenly books is to remind and bestow dignity upon human beings. "فیه ذكركم" (fīhi dhikrukum).

4- The past history of humanity, its destiny, and its future are outlined in the Quran. "فیه ذكركم" (fīhi dhikrukum).

5- The Arab nation and the land of Hijaz became honored and renowned through the blessing of the Quran. "فیه ذكركم" (fīhi dhikrukum).

6- If human beings reflect, they will certainly extend their hands in need toward revelation and the heavenly Book. "أفلا تعقلون" (Afalā ta‘qilūn - Will you not reason?).

7- We should utilize people's innate inclinations for their guidance. (Everyone desires their name to remain in history with goodness and dignity; Allah says in this Ayah: We sent down a Book for you so that your name may endure.) "فیه ذكركم" (fīhi dhikrukum).

8- Denying the Prophets is foolishness, because the teachings of the Quran are in harmony with reason. "انزلنا كتاباً ... أفلا تعقلون" (Anzalnā kitāban... afalā ta‘qilūn).

14) The tanwin (nunation) in the word "كتاباً" (kitāban) is a sign of majesty and greatness.

Ayah

وَكَمْ قَصَمْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ كَانَتْ ظَالِمَةً وَأَنشَأْنَا بَعْدَهَا قَوْمًا ءَاخَرِينَ (۱۱)
How many a town We have smashed that had been wrongdoing, and We brought forth another people after it. (11)

Messages

1- Destruction following oppression is a Divine tradition. «كم»

2- Whoever breaks the Divine law will be broken. «قصمنا» (The word qasam [قصم] means severe shattering.)

3- Humans themselves determine their own destiny. «قصمنا - ظالمة»

4- If oppression becomes a continuous practice and way of life, it will entail Divine wrath. «كانت ظالمة» (However, if the oppression is not continuous and the person repents upon realizing it, that wrath will not follow.)

5- Destroying and creating are no different for Allah. «قصمنا... انشأنا»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّآ أَحَسُّوا۟ بَأْسَنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنْهَا يَرْكُضُونَ (۱۲)
So when they sighted Our punishment, behold, they ran away from it. (12)
لَا تَرْكُضُوا۟ وَٱرْجِعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ مَآ أُتْرِفْتُمْ فِيهِ وَمَسَـٰكِنِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْـَٔلُونَ (۱۳)
‘Do not run away! Return to the opulence you were given to enjoy and to your dwellings so that you may be questioned!’ (13)
قَالُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۱٤)
They said, ‘Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’ (14)
فَمَا زَالَت تِّلْكَ دَعْوَىٰهُمْ حَتَّىٰ جَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ حَصِيدًا خَـٰمِدِينَ (۱٥)
That remained their cry until We turned them into a mown field, stilled [like burnt ashes]. (15)

Key Points

The word "حَصید" (haṣīd) means maḥṣūd, which is something harvested or reaped. The word "خامد" (khāmid) means to be extinguished or silenced. Regarding what is meant by "you will be questioned," it might mean that you question one another, or the poor question you, or future history questions you. And Allah is the Knower (واللَّه العالم).

Messages

1- The punishment that descends upon oppressive societies is gradual. "فلَمّا اَحَسّوا" (When they perceived it) (Perception relates to seeing the signs of wrath, which appear gradually).

2- No one has the power to resist the wrath of Allah. "یركضون" (They flee) (Even the arrogant abandon the scene and flee when danger strikes.)

3- One cannot escape Divine wrath. "لا تركضوا" (Do not flee).

4- Prosperity can pave the way for oppression and transgression. "ما اترفتم فیه" (in which you indulged in luxury).

5- Punishment must be carried out at the site of the crime, so that perhaps they may be questioned. "لعلكم تسئلون" (so that you may be questioned).

6- Oppressors will ultimately be held accountable. "تسئلون" (you will be questioned).

7- The end of oppression is regret. "یا ویلنا" (Woe to us!).

8- Eventually, dormant consciences will awaken, and deniers of the truth will confess to it. "انّا كنّا ظالمین" (Indeed, we were wrongdoers).

9- When calamity descends, cries lose their effect. "فما زالت تلك دعوئهم ..." (And that declaration of theirs did not cease...).

10- Disbelief and oppression bring about a fate for man like that of harvested, lifeless plants. "حصیداً خامدین" (harvested, extinguished).

11- Propaganda clamor, praises and flattery, and threats and intimidation are doomed to silence in the presence of Allah. "خامدین" (extinguished).

12- Continuous oppression leads to Divine punishment. "انّا كنا ظالمین... حصیداً خامدین" (Indeed, we were wrongdoers... harvested, extinguished).

Ayahs

وَمَا خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا لَـٰعِبِينَ (۱٦)
We did not create the sky and the earth and whatever is between them for play. (16)
لَوْ أَرَدْنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ لَهْوًا لَّٱتَّخَذْنَـٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّآ إِن كُنَّا فَـٰعِلِينَ (۱۷)
Had We desired to take up some diversion We would have taken it up with Ourselves, were We to do [so]. (17)

Key Points

The word "لو" (law) in the Arabic language is used for impossible actions, and since lahw (amusement/diversion) is impossible for Allah here, that word has been employed. In these verses, Allah first states: We are not "players" (la'ib), and then, instead of saying, "لو اردنا أن نتّخذ لعباً" (If We had intended to take a plaything), He said, "أن نتّخذ لهواً" (to take an amusement/diversion). Therefore, it becomes clear that the essence of lahw (amusement) and la'ib (play) is one. Thus, we read in Tafsir al-Mizan: A structured and organized action performed with imaginary goals, like the actions of children, is called la'ib (play). However, when these imaginary actions become serious enough to distract a person from essential matters and turn into a pastime, they are called lahw (amusement/diversion). Both of these are meaningless and void when attributed to Allah.

The Holy Quran repeatedly emphasizes that the universe is not a plaything but has been created with a purpose. For instance, Ayah 12 of Surah At-Talaq identifies the purpose as the faith of mankind, stating: "اللّه الّذى خلق سبع سموات و من الارض مثلهنّ ... لتعلموا انّ اللّه على كل شى ء قدیر وانّ اللّه قداحاط بكل شى ء عِلما" (Allah is He Who created seven heavens and of the earth the like thereof... so that you may know that Allah is over all things competent and that Allah has encompassed all things in [His] knowledge). Allah created the seven heavens and the like thereof of the earth... so that you may know that He is capable of everything and His knowledge encompasses everything. Of course, the realization of this goal is achieved through contemplation of existence, learning lessons, and acknowledging it.

Two meanings have been stated regarding the phrase, "If We had intended to take an amusement for Ourselves, We would have certainly done so from Our presence [or, from Our own accord]": A: The creation of existence, even if hypothetically for amusement, is His work, not that of the idols. B: If We hypothetically intended amusement, We would have created amusement from the angels and those close to Our court, and would have had nothing to do with the world of matter and nature.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, by citing this Ayah, forbade man from engaging in acts of lahw (diversion/amusement). (15)

15) دعائم الاسلام، ج 2، ص 206.

Messages

1- Existence has a purpose. "و ما خلقنا ... لاعبین" (And We did not create... as players). (If creation is not futile, then death is also not considered non-existence and the end of the matter.)

2- One whose goal is playfulness creates playthings, not an infinite universe whose mere cell and atom have compelled generations and ages to study them. "و ما بینهما لاعبین" (And what is between them, as players).

3- If, hypothetically, Allah's intention was play and futile action, He would have created it commensurate with His essence (not material things and bodies). (16) "لا تّخذناه من لدنّا" (We would have taken it from Our presence).

4- If, hypothetically, Allah had the goal of playfulness, He would have had nothing to do with the creation of man, He would not have put all these Prophets to trouble, He would not have commanded and forbidden, and He would not have ordained reward and punishment. "لو اردنا أن نتّخذ لهواً لاتّخذناه من لدنّا" (If We had intended to take an amusement, We would have taken it from Our presence).

16) تفسیر صافى.

Ayah

بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِٱلْحَقِّ عَلَى ٱلْبَـٰطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُۥ فَإِذَا هُوَ زَاهِقٌ ۚ وَلَكُمُ ٱلْوَيْلُ مِمَّا تَصِفُونَ (۱۸)
Indeed, We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it crushes its head, and behold, falsehood vanishes! And woe to you for what you allege [about Allah]. (18)

Key Points

The word qadhf (قَذف) means throwing from a distance with speed and power. The word damgh (دَمغ) refers to a blow that strikes the head and affects the brain. The word wayl (وَیل) means torment and destruction, and it is used in a situation where one deserves destruction.(17)

17) Lisān al-ʿArab.

Messages

1- The victory of truth is the will and desire of Allah and a manifestation of the purposeful nature of the system of creation. «بل نقذف بالحقّ»

2- The confrontation between truth and falsehood must be offensive, not defensive. «نقذف»

3- Allah's tradition (Sunnah) is based on the destruction and obliteration of falsehood. «نقذف - فیدمغه»

4- Truth is victorious over falsehood. «فاذا هو زاهق»

5- The obliteration of falsehood occurs through the presence of truth on the battlefield. «نقذف بالحقّ على الباطل»

6- In the struggle against falsehood, speed, power, and accurate targeting must be observed. «نقذف - فیدمغه»

7- Falsehood must be completely destroyed, not merely left half-alive. «یدمغه»

8- Falsehood is bound to vanish. «فاذا هو زاهق»

9- The temporary rule of falsehood is based on the divine tradition of granting respite, and the ultimate outcome belongs to the righteous (Muttaqin) and the people of truth. «فاذا هو زاهق»

10- Woe to those who consider creation a mere plaything. «و لكم الویل ممّا تصفون»

Ayahs

وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَنْ عِندَهُۥ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِۦ وَلَا يَسْتَحْسِرُونَ (۱۹)
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and those who are near Him do not disdain to worship Him, nor do they become weary. (19)
يُسَبِّحُونَ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ لَا يَفْتُرُونَ (۲٠)
They glorify [Him] night and day, and they do not flag. (20)
أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ ءَالِهَةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ هُمْ يُنشِرُونَ (۲۱)
Have they taken gods from the earth who raise [the dead]? (21)

Key Points

In metaphorical ownership, the closer a person gets to the master (Mawla), the less accountable he feels, and thus sometimes he commits transgressions. But in true ownership, the closer a person gets to the Worshipped One, the more he worships.(18) Regarding "و من عنده لا یستكبرون," it is stated in the first sermon of Nahj al-Balaghah concerning the angels: "لا یغشاهم نوم العیون و لا سهو العقول ولافترة الابدان ولا غفلة النسیان" Meaning, sleep, forgetfulness, fatigue, and negligence do not affect the angels.

18) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The true ownership (Mulkiyyah) of the universe belongs solely to Allah. "و له مَن فى..." (Therefore, since we are His possessions, let us fulfill our duty of servitude.)

2- Worship and servitude are not exclusive to humans. "و مَن عنده ..."

3- There are conscious beings in the heavens. (The word "مَن" [man] in the phrase "مَن فى السّموات," which is contrasted with "مَن عنده," indicates conscious beings other than the angels.)

4- Some of Allah's creatures attain the station of proximity (Qurb) to Him. "و من عنده"

5- The root of abandoning worship is arrogance (Takabbur). "لا یستكبرون عن عبادته"

6- Worship aligns humans with the angels. "لا یستكبرون عن عبادته"

7- Vigor (or enthusiasm) is the source of the value of worship. "لا یستحسرون"

8- Angels do not sleep. "یسبحون اللیل والنّهار"

9- The continuity of worship gives it value. "یسبحون اللیل والنّهار - لا یفترون"

10- We must question false beliefs. "أم اتّخذوا آلهة من الارض هم ینشرون"

Ayah

لَوْ كَانَ فِيهِمَآ ءَالِهَةٌ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَا ۚ فَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَرْشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۲۲)
Had there been any gods in them other than Allah, they would surely have fallen apart. Clear is Allah, the Lord of the Throne, of what they allege [concerning Him]. (22)

Key Points

If we look closely at a book written by several authors, we will clearly see that the sentences, style, and content of each section of the book differ from the others. It is obvious that the larger and more detailed the book, the greater and more apparent this difference will be. This is because, even if accidental coordination is found in writing a single sentence, line, or page, such harmony and order in the great book of existence is impossible without a single, unique Lord. Question: If a single God is the source of all affairs, then what is the reason for the existing contradictions? Answer: If they can even be called contradictions, they must be said to be the type of contradiction found between the two blades of a pair of scissors or the two pans of a scale, which operate under a single management and for the achievement of a single goal. Alternatively, they are like the opposing movement of a person's two hands when squeezing a cloth to wring out its water. Hisham ibn Hakam asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: What is the proof of Allah's Oneness? The Imam replied: «اتّصال التّدبیر وتمام الصنع كما قال اللّه عزّوجلّ: «لوكان فیهما...»» The continuity and coherence of the management of the universe and the perfection of creation are the proofs of His Oneness. (19) This Ayah is in response to those who believed in a separate Lord (Rabb), manager (Mudabbir), and god for every thing and every affair. Therefore, He said: «ربّ العرش» meaning the Lord of all existence is Allah.

19) Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 250.

Messages

1- The multiplicity of Lords is an impossible matter. «لوكان» (The word "لَو" (if) is used in a context where the action is impossible to occur.)

2- The God of the heavens and the earth is one. «فیهما» Contrary to the polytheists who believed in a separate Lord (Rabb) for each of the heavens and the earth, the Quran considers the Lord of all to be one. «و هوالّذى فى السماء اله و فى الارض اله» (20)

3- Multiplicity in management leads to disintegration and corruption. «آلهة - لفسدتا»

4- When mentioning false speech, the glorification (Tanzih) of Allah is necessary. «لوكان فیهما - فسبحان اللّه»

5- The glorification (Tasbih) of Allah must follow awareness and be based on reasoning and logic, not blindly or merely by the movement of the tongue. «لوكان فیهما - فسبحان اللّه» (First, the argument was presented that if there were two managements, existence would fall into corruption; then the glorification of God followed. «سبحان اللّه عمّا یصفون») 20) Al-Zukhruf, 84.

Ayah

لَا يُسْـَٔلُ عَمَّا يَفْعَلُ وَهُمْ يُسْـَٔلُونَ (۲۳)
He is not questioned concerning what He does, but they are questioned. (23)

Key Points

The responsibility of accountability for the actions man performs has been raised repeatedly in the Quran, including: «فَوَ ربّك لنسئلنّهم اجمعین»(21) (By your Lord, We shall certainly question them all.) Or in another place, we read: «و قفوهم انّهم مسئولون»(22) (Stop them; they must be questioned.) In any case, on the Day of Judgment, questions will be asked about thoughts and intentions, youth and lifespan, income and expenditure, the choice of leader, and obedience to elders. Question: How is it that in another Ayah He says that no jinn or human will be questioned? «فیومئذٍ لا یسئل عن ذنبه انس ولا جان»(23) Answer: The scenes on the Day of Judgment are different, and each scene has its own specific conditions. For example, in one station (Mawqif), when humans are questioned, they can respond verbally, but in another station, their lips will be sealed, and other limbs and organs will testify to their deeds. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Allah addressed Adam عليه السلام: «یابن آدم، بمشیّتى كنت انت الّذى تشاء لنفسك ما تشاء» meaning, I have willed that you be free to will for yourself whatever you wish.(24)

21) Hujr, 92. 22) Saffat, 24. 23) Rahman, 39. 24) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Allah is the true and absolute Owner of existence, and an owner is not held accountable for His disposal of what He owns. «ربّ العرش... لا یسئل»

2- Only someone who has a claim or a right can question, and no one has a claim against Allah or a right over Him. «لا یسئل»

3- Only someone who is superior to Him and above Him can hold Allah accountable, and no such being exists. «لا یسئل»

4- Humans are responsible and committed regarding their actions. «و هم یسئلون»

5- Questioning and accountability are the best signs of responsibility and the most important indication of human freedom and choice. «وهم یسئلون» (Indeed, a compelled person is not held accountable, because questioning an individual who is compelled and lacks free will is rationally repugnant.)

Ayah

أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةً ۖ قُلْ هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذَا ذِكْرُ مَن مَّعِىَ وَذِكْرُ مَن قَبْلِى ۗ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ٱلْحَقَّ ۖ فَهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۲٤)
Have they taken gods besides Him? Say, ‘Produce your evidence! This is a precept of those who are with me, and a precept of those [who went] before me.’ But most of them do not know the truth, and so they are disregardful. (24)

Key Points

Ali (عليه السلام) said: Indeed, with the revelation of the Quran to the Prophet, the knowledge of the previous prophets, the knowledge of the successors (Awsiya), and the knowledge of what will occur until the Day of Judgment, has been granted. Then the Imam recited this Ayah, where Allah says to His Prophet: (25) «هذا ذكر مَن معى و ذكر مَن قبلى...» Therefore, the Quran contains the knowledge of what was (mā kān) and what will be (mā yakūn).

25) Tafsir Furat, p. 261.

Messages

1- Whoever distances himself from the One God (Allah) falls into the trap of multiple gods. «أم اتّخذوا من دونه آلهة»

2- The Prophet must deal with people through argumentation and demand proof from them. «قل هاتوا برهانكم»

3- Monotheism (Tawhid) possesses proof, «لوكان فیهما...» but polytheism (Shirk) has no proof whatsoever. (26) «هاتوا برهانكم»

4- In the fundamentals of belief (Usul al-Din), one cannot practice blind imitation (taqlid). «هاتوا برهانكم»

5- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the message of all Divine Books. «ذكر مَن معى وذكر مَن قبلى»

6- Divine Books are the means of reviving dormant innate nature (fitrah) and reminding people of forgotten knowledge. «ذكر من معى و ذكر من قبلى »

7- In an atmosphere of ignorance, neither the acceptance of the majority nor their rejection holds value. «اكثرهم لا یعلمون الحقّ فهم معرضون» 26) As we read in another Ayah: «و من یدع مع اللّه الهاً آخر لا برهان له» (Al-Mu'minun, 117).

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا نُوحِىٓ إِلَيْهِ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعْبُدُونِ (۲٥)
We did not send any apostle before you but that We revealed to him that ‘There is no god except Me; so worship Me.’ (25)

Key Points

Although Allah explicitly stated the issue of the finality (Khātamiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله in Ayah 40 of Surah Al-Ahzab, where He said: «ما كان محمّد اَبا احدٍ من رجالكم ولكن رسول اللّه و خاتم النبیین», He has also alluded to it through various other means. Among these is that the phrase «من قبلك» (from before you) is mentioned more than 30 times in various expressions, but the phrase «من بعدك» (from after you) has not been mentioned even once.

Messages

1- Prophethood (Risālah) is accompanied by the reception of revelation (Wahy). «ما ارسلنا ... من رسول الاّ نوحى الیه»

2- Monotheism (Tawhīd) has been at the forefront of the program of all Divine Prophets. «ما ارسلنا ... الاّ نوحى الیه انّه لا اله الاّ انا»

3- Action must be based on belief: first the foundation (Mabnā), then the structure (Binā). (First, Monotheism in Divinity (Ulūhiyyah), then Monotheism in Worship ('Ibādah)). «لا اله الاّ انا، فاعبدون»

4- The exclusivity of Truth (Haqq) against falsehood (Bātil) is a Divine slogan. «لا اله الاّ انا»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۚ بَلْ عِبَادٌ مُّكْرَمُونَ (۲٦)
They say, ‘The All-beneficent has taken offsprings.’ Immaculate is He! Indeed, they are [His] honoured servants. (26)
لَا يَسْبِقُونَهُۥ بِٱلْقَوْلِ وَهُم بِأَمْرِهِۦ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲۷)
They do not venture to speak ahead of Him, and they act by His command. (27)

Key Points

The idea that angels are the children of God was prevalent not only among the polytheists but also among the Jews and Christians. (27) The application of the word mukramūn (honored ones) to the angels is due to the fact that, firstly, they are servants of Allah, and secondly, this servitude is accompanied by sincerity (Ikhlās). The word al-Raḥmān (the Most Compassionate) was a known name for God among the polytheists. «قالوا اتّخذ الرّحمن» (They said, "The Most Compassionate has taken...") The conditions governing man affect his beliefs and judgments. Since human beings possess a body and see themselves as needing children, they think similarly about Allah, Who is free from corporeality and need.

27) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- When quoting the blasphemous speech of others, we must declare Allah's transcendence (Tanzīh). «ولداً سبحانه»

2- Whenever we mention the deviations of others, we should also state the path of truth. «وقالوا - بل»

3- Submission to the command of Allah is the secret to the servant's honor (karāmah). «مكرمون - لا یسبقون»

(Indeed, those who are honored are those who do not precede Allah's command, neither in word nor in deed.) «مكرمون - لا یسبقون»

4- Angels are divine agents who act only by His command and never precede His orders. «لا یسبقونه - بامره یعملون»

Ayah

يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلَا يَشْفَعُونَ إِلَّا لِمَنِ ٱرْتَضَىٰ وَهُم مِّنْ خَشْيَتِهِۦ مُشْفِقُونَ (۲۸)
He knows that which is before them and that which is behind them, and they do not intercede except for someone He approves of, and they are apprehensive for the fear of Him. (28)

Key Points

Khawf (خوف) means fear of sin, but Khashyah (خشیت) means fear of Divine Majesty, a fear that is accompanied by reverence and respect. It is stated in narrations that the intent of "مَن ارتضى" (whom He approves) is one whose religion is approved, even if he is a sinner, because the believing person, due to the repentance he performs after the sin, is granted favor and becomes subject to intercession (Shafa'ah). (28)

28) Tafasir Safi and Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid al-Saduq p. 408.

Messages

1- Unquestioning submission is valuable before a God Who is aware of everything. "بامره یعملون, یعلم" (Attention to the fact that Allah knows everything leads to submission accompanied by khashyah.)

2- Allah's knowledge regarding the past and the future is uniform, unlike human knowledge which is only regarding the past. "یعلم ما بین ایدیهم و ما خلفهم"

3- Servitude and submission before Allah are the cause of possessing the station of intercession (Shafa'ah). "عبادٌ مكرمون, لا یسبقونه, یشفعون"

4- Angels are also among those who intercede. "لا یشفعون الاّ لمَن ارتضى"

5- Intercession requires merit. Those who become subject to intercession are those whose religion Allah is pleased with. "الاّ لمَنِ ارتضى"

6- Greater knowledge of Allah must lead to greater khashyah. "و هم من خشیته مشفقون" (The station of infallibility (Ismah) does not prevent khashyah of Allah.)

Ayah

۞ وَمَن يَقُلْ مِنْهُمْ إِنِّىٓ إِلَـٰهٌ مِّن دُونِهِۦ فَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِيهِ جَهَنَّمَ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲۹)
Should any of them say, ‘I am a god besides Him,’ We will requite him with hell. Thus do We requite the wrongdoers. (29)

Key Points

This Ayah might be based on the principle of "speak to the door so the wall hears," meaning, O people, know that whoever—even if it were the honored angels (though this notion is impossible concerning them)—invites others to polytheism (shirk), they must await the punishment of Hellfire.

Messages

1- The claim of polytheism is neither acceptable nor forgivable from any creature. «و من یقل منهم»

2- Whoever stands up against Allah and presents himself as a partner, whether he gains followers or not, his punishment is Hell. «انىّ اله - نجزیه جهنّم»

3- Divine punishments follow an eternal tradition and law. «كذلك»

4- The polytheist is an oppressor (ẓālim), and the punishment for oppression is nothing but Hellfire. «نجزى الظالمین»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ كَانَتَا رَتْقًا فَفَتَقْنَـٰهُمَا ۖ وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ حَىٍّ ۖ أَفَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳٠)
Have the faithless not regarded that the heavens and the earth were interwoven and We unravelled them, and We made every living thing out of water? Will they not then have faith? (30)

Key Points

Regarding what is meant by the heavens and the earth being closed ("رتقاً") and then being opened ("ففتقناها"), several interpretations have been mentioned: A: The earth and the heavens were a single entity at the beginning of creation, which was a massive cloud of gases. Then, due to internal explosions and movements, they gradually separated from each other. B: The fundamental materials of the heavens and the earth were initially one, but over time, they took on a new form. C: The meaning of the heavens being closed is the lack of rain, and the meaning of the earth being closed is the lack of vegetation. Allah opened them; He sent down rain from the sky and caused plants to grow from the earth. (29) Water is the source of the creation of humans and animals. 70% of the bodies of humans and animals consist of water. The life of plants depends on water.

29) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Observing nature with purpose is the key to acquiring faith. «أَوَلَمْ يَرَ ... أَفَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ»

2- The natural world has gone through different eras. «كَانَتَا رَتْقًا فَفَتَقْنَاهُمَا»

3- There is no good in stagnation and closure; opening is the source of life. «رَتْقًا فَفَتَقْنَاهُمَا»

4- The source of all living creatures is water. «وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ الْمَاءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍّ»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ رَوَٰسِىَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِهِمْ وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا فِجَاجًا سُبُلًا لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (۳۱)
We set firm mountains in the earth lest it should shake with them, and We made broad ways in them so that they may be guided [to their destinations]. (31)

Key Points

The word rawāsi (رواسى) is the plural of rāsiya (راسیه), meaning stable or fixed, and what is meant by it in this Ayah are the steadfast mountains. The word tamīd (تَمید) is derived from mayd (مید), meaning the agitation or trembling of large things. The word fijāj (فجاج) refers to wide paths between two mountains, just as the narrow and tight paths between mountains are called shi'b (شعب).(30) Many benefits have been mentioned for mountains, including: retaining snow and storing water for the summers, preventing strong winds caused by air displacement, providing a suitable environment for the growth of plants and animals, yielding various types of stones for building construction, and... How can it be believed that the Wise Allah placed mountains to prevent the earth from shaking, but did not place patient and strong guardians (awliyā) and leaders (pīshwāyān) to prevent humans from slipping in the face of calamities?!

30) Qāmūs.

Messages

1- The creation of mountains is not accidental, but is based on Divine planning. j'alnā (جعلنا)

2- Mountains are a source of tranquility for the earth. Without mountains, the earth is exposed to severe tremors due to internal gases and molten materials. rawāsiya an tamīda bihim (رواسِىَ أن تمیدبهم)

3- Even amidst great obstacles, there are paths to salvation. j'alnā fīhā fijājan (جعلنا فیها فجاجا)

4- The paths between the mountains are both a means of accessing other regions and a factor guiding (one) to Divine wisdom. la'allahum yahtadūn (لعلّهم یهتدون)

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ سَقْفًا مَّحْفُوظًا ۖ وَهُمْ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِهَا مُعْرِضُونَ (۳۲)
We made the sky a preserved roof and yet they are disregardful of its signs. (32)

Key Points

| The Earth possesses two types of protection: one from within and the other from without. One is by means of the mountains, protecting against the movements and pressure of internal gases that cause severe earthquakes and tremors. And the other is by means of the sky's air and the atmosphere surrounding the Earth, protecting against the daily bombardment of meteorites, the deadly rays of sunlight, and lethal cosmic radiation that constantly expose the Earth to the most dangerous threats. Perhaps the intent behind the sky being "preserved" (محفوظ) is the protection of the heavens from the coming and going of devils (Shayatin), just as He says elsewhere: «و حفظناها من كلّ شیطان رجیم»(31)

31) Al-Hijr, 17.

Messages

  1. The creation of the sky is based on calculation and purpose. «جعلنا السماء سقفاً محفوظاً»
  2. Not only the Earth, but the heavens are also preserved. «محفوظا» (They are preserved from falling upon mankind.)
  3. The sky is both a protector «سقفاً» and protected. «محفوظا»
  4. The sky contains signs of Divine power and wisdom. «آیاتها»
  5. Although Allah emphasizes taking heed and accepting guidance from His signs and tokens of power, the heedless and arrogant human turns away from them. «عن آیاتها معرضون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ كُلٌّ فِى فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ (۳۳)
It is He who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon, each swimming in an orbit. (33)

Key Points

Night, just like the day, is a creation and a thing made by Allah, similar to the verse which states: «خلق الموت و الحیاة» (32), meaning that death, like life, possesses an existence that Allah has created. Regarding the word «كلّ» (all) in this verse, there are several possibilities: A: Not only the sun and the moon, but all celestial bodies are in motion. B: The place of rising of the sun and the moon is multiple at different times (33). C: Night and day are also in motion, just like the sun and the moon, because if we look at the Earth from outside its space, we will see the night, which is the shadow of the Earth, in the form of a cone, and the day in the shape of a bright cylinder, rotating around the Earth (34).

32) Al-Mulk, 2. 33) Tafsir Lahiji. 34) Tafasir Al-Mizan and Namuneh.

Messages

1- The best way to know Allah is to pay close attention to His divine creations. «هو الّذى خلق...» This is because nature is always accessible to all human beings.

2- All celestial bodies possess an orbit in which they move. «فى كلّ فلك...»

Ayahs

وَمَا جَعَلْنَا لِبَشَرٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ ٱلْخُلْدَ ۖ أَفَإِي۟ن مِّتَّ فَهُمُ ٱلْخَـٰلِدُونَ (۳٤)
We did not give immortality to any human before you. If you are fated to die, will they live on forever? (34)
كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۗ وَنَبْلُوكُم بِٱلشَّرِّ وَٱلْخَيْرِ فِتْنَةً ۖ وَإِلَيْنَا تُرْجَعُونَ (۳٥)
Every soul shall taste death, and We will test you with good and ill by way of test, and to Us you will be brought back. (35)

Key Points

Imam Ali (عليه السلام) said regarding "نبلوكم بالشرّ و الخیر" (We test you with evil and good): Health and wealth are good (خير), and sickness and poverty are evil (شرّ), and both are for the purpose of testing. (35)

Portrait of Death in the Quran and Narrations

1- Readiness for death is a sign of the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah). «اِن زَعمتم انّكم اولیاء للّه من دون الناس فتمنّوا الموت» (36) 2- The place of death in human life is like a necklace upon the chest of young women. «خطّ الموت على وُلد آدم مَخطّ القلاده على جید الفتاة» (37) 3- Death is not the end of the road, but rather it is equivalent to a change in life and lifestyle, like clothing that is changed. (38) 4- Every human being departs from this world in a certain way; some in the most difficult states, and others like smelling a flower. (39) 5- Man's fear of death is like the fear of a driver who either lacks fuel "آهٍ من قلّة الزّاد و طول الطریق" (40), or is carrying smuggled goods and prohibited items (meaning he is a sinner), or is not ready to drive. Otherwise, why the fear?

35) Mustadrak, Vol. 2, p. 149. 36) Surah Al-Jumu'ah, 6. 37) This simile is from Imam Husayn (عليه السلام) on the eve of his journey to Karbala. 38) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 6, p. 154. 39) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 6, p. 152. 40) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 77.

Messages

1- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is that man is not eternal in this world. «ما جعلنا لبشر... الخلد»

2- If any human were meant to live forever in this world, that person would be the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله). «أفاین متّ فهم الخالدون»

3- Although good (خير) and evil (شرّ) are both tools for testing, testing through hardships is more appropriate. «نبلوكم بالشر والخیر» (Perhaps the precedence of "شرّ" is due to this appropriateness.)

4- Divine tests are difficult and significant. «نبلوكم... فتنه»

5- We are not annihilated or destroyed by death. «الینا ترجعون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَءَاكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى يَذْكُرُ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَهُم بِذِكْرِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ هُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۳٦)
Whenever the faithless see you they only take you in derision: ‘Is this the one who remembers your gods?’ while they remain defiant towards the remembrance of the All-beneficent. (36)

Key Points

Disbelief (Kufr) leads man to a point where he mocks strong logic and the word of the infallible (Ma'soom), yet he is unwilling to tolerate the slightest disrespect towards stone and wood. Therefore, they said: «یذكر الهتكم» (He mentions your gods), while their intention was «یذكر الهتكم بسوء» (He mentions your gods badly/with evil). If a foul-smelling carcass falls into a pool or reservoir of water, the stench and foulness increase with the coming of rain. Disbelief is like a dead carcass in the human soul, which shows greater obstinacy upon seeing the Prophet.

Messages

1- The disbelievers possess no scientific or logical weapon other than mockery and humiliation. «اِن یتّخذونك...» (Mockery is a sign of inability to reason.)

2- The disbelievers incited the emotions of the people against the Prophet. «آلهتكم»

3- Whoever is afflicted with inner malice, the more he sees the Divine leaders, the more he regresses. «رأك - یتّخذونك»

Ayah

خُلِقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مِنْ عَجَلٍ ۚ سَأُو۟رِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِى فَلَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونِ (۳۷)
Man is a creature of haste. Soon I will show you My signs. So do not ask Me to hasten. (37)

Messages

1- The haste of humans in their requests has no effect on the Divine Will and Wisdom. «سأوریكم آیاتى فلاتستعجلون»

2- Man is capable of controlling and restraining his instincts. «خلق الانسان من عجل ... فلاتستعجلون»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۳۸)
And they say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’ (38)

Messages

1- Man's characteristic of haste is the basis for the disbelievers' questioning about the time of the fulfillment of the Divine promises. «خلق الانسان من عجل ... یقولون متى هذا الوعد»

2- The disbelievers deemed the Prophet of Islam and his followers to be liars. «ان كنتم صادقین»

Ayah

لَوْ يَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ حِينَ لَا يَكُفُّونَ عَن وُجُوهِهِمُ ٱلنَّارَ وَلَا عَن ظُهُورِهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (۳۹)
If only the faithless knew about the time when they will not be able to keep the Fire off their faces and their backs, nor will they be helped! (39)

Key Points

This verse refers to an example of human haste, which is that the disbelievers constantly asked the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: When is the promised Day of Judgment and the time when we will be punished for our disbelief? By this, they caused distress to the Prophet. Allah consoles the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله (by stating) that if they were aware of the Day when fire will encompass them, not only from the front and the back, but from all sides, and they will have no way to escape, they would not be hasty for its arrival.

Messages

1- Human haste is due to ignorance. «لویعلم...» (If they knew, they would not rush.)

2- Knowledge of the dangers of the Day of Judgment prevents hostility towards the truth. «لو یعلم ...»

3- Disbelief prevents intercession. «ولاهم ینصرون»

4- Other deities lack any effectiveness. «ولاهم ینصرون»

5- On the Day of Judgment, the disbelievers will neither have the power themselves to ward off the fire, nor will any help come to them from idols or tyrants (Taghuts). «ولاهم ینصرون»

Ayah

بَلْ تَأْتِيهِم بَغْتَةً فَتَبْهَتُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ رَدَّهَا وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ (٤٠)
Indeed, it will overtake them suddenly, dumbfounding them. So they will neither be able to avert it, nor will they be granted any respite. (40)

Messages

1- When thought is disrupted, ability is also disrupted. (Intellectual bewilderment takes away power.) «فتبهتهم فلا یستطیعون»

2- Granting respite to the disbelievers is a Divine tradition (Sunnah) in this world, not the Hereafter. «ولاهم ینظرون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدِ ٱسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٤۱)
Apostles were certainly derided before you; but those who ridiculed them were besieged by what they had been deriding. (41)

Messages

1- Humanity throughout history has benefited from the prophets. «برسل من قبلك»

2- The method of the disbelievers throughout history has been mockery and ridicule. «سخروا منهم»

3- Awareness of the suffering of others alleviates one's own suffering. «استهزى ء برسل من قبلك»

4- Wise actions should not be abandoned due to the mockery of a group. (Although the prophets were mocked, the sending of prophets was not halted.) «استهزى ء برسل من قبلك»

5- Mockery is a cause of affliction. «سخروا ... فحاق ...»

6- Punishment is commensurate with the sin. «فحاق بالّذین سخروا... ما كانوا یستهزءؤن»

Ayah

قُلْ مَن يَكْلَؤُكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ مِنَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ۗ بَلْ هُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِم مُّعْرِضُونَ (٤۲)
Say, ‘Who can guard you, day and night, against [the punishment of] the All-beneficent [should He want to punish you]?’ Indeed, they are disregardful of their Lord’s remembrance. (42)

Messages

1- Powers cannot protect humanity from dangers. «قل مَن یكلؤكم...»

2- Humanity constantly needs divine protection and support. «مَن یكلؤكم...»

3- One of the successful methods of propagation is addressing consciences. «قل مَن یكلؤكم...»

4- Allah's protection is constant and extends to everyone, even the disbelievers (kuffar). «مَن یكلؤكم»

5- Our deeds are such that they even provoke the source of Mercy (Ar-Rahman) to wrath. «من الرّحمن» (His protection is based on kindness and mercy) «یكلؤكم... من الرحمن»

6- Protection is among the attributes of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «یكلؤكم - ربّهم» (From the protection of the heavens to the docility of animals, to the defense against microbes entering the body, hundreds of types of protection exist for humanity at every moment).

7- If people turn away from the remembrance of Allah, He will also turn away from them. Initially, Allah addressed the disbelievers directly «یكلؤكم», but later, it is as if He turned away from them and referred to them in the third person (as absent). «بل هم»

8- It is necessary for a person to remember his true Sustainer (Rabb); otherwise, he deserves reproach. «بل هم عن ذكر ربّهم معرضون»

Ayah

أَمْ لَهُمْ ءَالِهَةٌ تَمْنَعُهُم مِّن دُونِنَا ۚ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَلَا هُم مِّنَّا يُصْحَبُونَ (٤۳)
Do they have gods besides Us to defend them? Neither can they help themselves, nor can they shield [the idolaters] from Us. (43)

Messages

1- That which is other than Allah neither has the internal ability to defend itself nor the external ability to help others. «لایستطیعون نصر...»

2- How can the needy be worshipped? «لا یستطیعون نصر انفسهم»

3- A being that has not received existence from the Bestower of Existence, how can it become the Bestower of Existence? «آلهة... لایستطیعون نصر انفسهم و لا هم منّا یُصحبون»

Ayah

بَلْ مَتَّعْنَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ طَالَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْعُمُرُ ۗ أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّا نَأْتِى ٱلْأَرْضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنْ أَطْرَافِهَآ ۚ أَفَهُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبُونَ (٤٤)
Indeed, We have provided for them and their fathers until they lived on for long years. Do they not see how We visit the land diminishing it at its edges? Are they the ones who will prevail? (44)

Key Points

Regarding the meaning of the phrase «ننقصها من اطرافها» (We reduce it from its borders/sides)—what the reduction of the earth's borders signifies—various interpretations have been mentioned: A: The intent is the death of scholars and their absence. (41) B: The purpose is the extinction of nations and the destruction of the earth's inhabitants. (42) C: With the expansion of Islam, the regions inhabited by disbelief (Kufr) are reduced. (43) D: The geographical volume of the earth is decreasing.

41) Tafsir Safi. 42) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 43) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Longevity and enjoyment are by the will of Allah, and no other deity plays a role in them. «متّعنا»

2- Not every enjoyment granted to humans and their ancestors is a sign of Divine favor. «بل متّعنا» (The long life and enjoyment of your ancestors should not make you heedless.)

3- Allah is not in a hurry to punish the disbelievers. «متّعنا»

4- One should not be pleased with every long life. «حتّى طال علیهم العمر» Allah has made the long life of the disbelievers a respite for the increase of their sins. «انّما نملى لهم لیزدادوا اثماً» (44)

5- Awareness of the history of nations and civilizations and taking lessons from them is praiseworthy. «أفلا یرون ...»

6- After observing the successive extinction of nations, do the disbelievers still assume victory? «ننقصها من اطرافها، أفهم الغالبون» 44) Aal-i-Imran, 178.

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أُنذِرُكُم بِٱلْوَحْىِ ۚ وَلَا يَسْمَعُ ٱلصُّمُّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا مَا يُنذَرُونَ (٤٥)
Say, ‘I indeed warn you by the means of revelation.’ But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned. (45)

Messages

1- The glad tidings and warnings of the prophets are not based on personal opinions, conjecture, speculation, or human fantasies, but rather are based on definite Divine revelation. «انّما انذركم بالوحى»

2- Those who do not make proper use of Divine blessings are, as it were, deprived of that blessing. «الصّم» Meaning, if someone outwardly has ears but does not hear the truth, or has eyes but does not see the realities, he is in fact both deaf and blind.

3- Sometimes the ineffectiveness of propagation is due to the people lacking receptivity, and not because there is a deficiency in the propagator or his method. «انذركم... لا یسمع»

4- Some are not capable of guidance and there is no hope for their guidance. «ولا یسمع الصم ...»

What good is preaching to the black-hearted? An iron nail does not penetrate stone.

Ayah

وَلَئِن مَّسَّتْهُمْ نَفْحَةٌ مِّنْ عَذَابِ رَبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ يَـٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (٤٦)
Should a whiff of your Lord’s punishment touch them, they will surely say, ‘Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’ (46)

Messages

1- Those who are not awakened by the warning and admonition of the prophets will be awakened by the whip of punishment. With the occurrence of the simplest dangers, all claimed prides are shattered, and dormant consciences awaken. «لئن مسّتهم نفحة»

2- The oppressors will one day confess to their injustice and oppression. «لیقولنّ»

3- Those who are indifferent to the warnings of the prophets are oppressors. «لایُسمع الصُمّ... انّاكنّا ظالمین»

Ayah

وَنَضَعُ ٱلْمَوَٰزِينَ ٱلْقِسْطَ لِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فَلَا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَإِن كَانَ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍ مِّنْ خَرْدَلٍ أَتَيْنَا بِهَا ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِنَا حَـٰسِبِينَ (٤۷)
We shall set up just scales on the Day of Resurrection, and no soul will be wronged in the least. Even if it be the weight of a mustard seed We shall produce it and We suffice as reckoners. (47)

Key Points

This Ayah is good tidings for the righteous and a warning for the transgressors, indicating that your deeds will be rewarded or punished without any reduction or addition. As Fayd Kashani says: Beware, for every atom is accounted for here; the court of reckoning and the book are here.

"Khardal" (mustard) is the name of a plant that has very tiny black seeds, and it has become proverbial for its smallness and insignificance.

"Mizan" (the Balance), meaning the means of measurement, has been mentioned repeatedly in the Holy Qur'an and emphasized by Allah the Almighty.

It is obvious that the measurement of everything is done with a tool appropriate for it. For example, to measure air temperature, it is measured with a thermometer; to measure length, it is measured with a meter; and to measure the weight of objects, it is calculated with a kilogram.

It is clear that to weigh man and his deeds, a tool appropriate for him—which is the Perfect Man—must be used. Just as it is stated in narrations: The infallible Imams, the Prophets, and the Successors علیهم السلام are the balances of justice (Mizan al-Qist). (45) And in the Ziyarat (visitation prayer) of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام, we read: "Peace be upon the Balance of Deeds (Mizan al-A'mal)." (46)

Question: If Allah's reckoning is truly this precise, then what is the place and justification for Divine pardon and generosity? Answer: Reckoning is one principle, and grace and forgiveness are another principle, each preserved in its proper place, and they do not contradict one another.

Question: Some Ayahs state that Allah will not set up a Balance for a group of people, "فلانقیم لهم یوم القیامة وزنا" (We shall not assign them any weight on the Day of Resurrection). Will the Balance be only for specific individuals? Answer: No, the Balance will be established for all human beings. However, since the Balance is for calculating deeds, when a transgressor has nothing to present and his consignment to Hell is certain and definite, there is no longer any need to establish the Balance, and measurement becomes meaningless. (47)

(45) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 469; Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 31. (46) Tafsir Namuneh. (47) Tafasir Safi and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Everything, every person, and every deed that is capable of being measured will have its own balance. "الموازین" (The Balances)

2- Allah's reckoning is extremely precise. "مثقال حبّة من خردل" (the weight of a mustard seed)

3- Our deeds in this world are not erased, and we will face them on the Day of Judgment. "أتینا بها" (We bring it forth)

4- Allah is both All-Knowing ("أتینا بها"), Just ("القسط"), and the Reckoner ("حاسبین").

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ٱلْفُرْقَانَ وَضِيَآءً وَذِكْرًا لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤۸)
Certainly We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, a light and reminder for the Godwary (48)
ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَهُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّاعَةِ مُشْفِقُونَ (٤۹)
—those who fear their Lord in secret, and who are apprehensive of the Hour. (49)

Key Points

Just as the general goals of the Prophets are one, the characteristics of their heavenly books are also uniform. The characteristics mentioned in this Ayah for the Book of the Torah have also been mentioned in other Ayahs for the Quran:

A: Furqan (Criterion), «تبارك الّذى نزّل الفرقان على عبده»(48) (Furqan refers to the news by which truth is distinguished from falsehood.) B: Dhiya' (Light), «و أنزل الیكم نوراً مبینا»(49) C: Dhikr (Reminder), «اِنّا نحن نزّلنا الذّكر»(50)

48) Al-Furqan, 1. 49) An-Nisa, 174. 50) Al-Hijr, 9.

Messages

1- It is not necessary for every Prophet to have a separate book; just as Moses and Aaron, both Prophets, had one book, which was the Torah. «آتینا موسى و هارون ...»

2- For success in any endeavor, a person needs several things:

A: Recognizing the right path from the wrong path. «الفرقان» B: A light by which he can see and move forward. «ضیاءً» C: Attention and focus on the goal, so that he does not deviate from the main path.(51) «ذكراً»

3- To benefit from the guidance of the Prophets, one must be equipped with the spirit of piety (taqwa). «ذكراً للمتّقین»

4- The sign of piety is the fear of God and concern regarding the Day of Judgment. «للمتّقین الّذین یخشون ربّهم... من السّاعة مشفقون»

51) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

وَهَـٰذَا ذِكْرٌ مُّبَارَكٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ ۚ أَفَأَنتُمْ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ (٥٠)
This [too] is a blessed reminder, which We have sent down. Will you then deny it? (50)

Key Points

It is sufficient proof of the blessedness of the Quran that, in a short period, it was able to bring many people from polytheism to monotheism, from division to unity, from ignorance to knowledge, from savagery to civilization, from captivity to leadership, from impurity to cleanliness, from poverty to sufficiency, from stagnation to movement, from superstitions to realities, from humiliation to dignity, from following the Taghut to obeying the Infallible, from heedlessness to awareness, from instability to firmness, from disbelief to faith, and, in a single word, from darknesses to light. In the previous Ayah, only the word "Dhikr" (Reminder) was used for the Torah, but in this Ayah, the Quran is considered a "ذكرٌ مباركٌ" (Blessed Reminder). Of course, the attributes of Furqan (Criterion) and Noor (Light) have also been mentioned for the Quran in other verses.

Messages

1- The Quran is a Book whose reminders can attract millions of hearts across different eras and generations. "ذكر مبارك" (Blessed Reminder).

2- The blessedness (Barakah) of the Quran is undeniable. "أفانتم له منكرون" (Are you then denying it?).

Ayahs

۞ وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ رُشْدَهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِۦ عَـٰلِمِينَ (٥۱)
Certainly We had given Abraham his probity before, and We knew him (51)
إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِيلُ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنتُمْ لَهَا عَـٰكِفُونَ (٥۲)
when he said to his father and his people, ‘What are these images to which you keep on clinging?’ (52)
قَالُوا۟ وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا لَهَا عَـٰبِدِينَ (٥۳)
They said, ‘We found our fathers worshipping them.’ (53)
قَالَ لَقَدْ كُنتُمْ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُمْ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (٥٤)
He said, ‘Certainly you and your fathers have been in manifest error.’ (54)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the discussion revolved around the Torah and the Quran, but in this verse, Allah says: sending Prophets and heavenly Books is not a new issue, and even before these, during the time of Abraham (عليه السلام), We had the program of inviting the misguided. By looking at the history of idol worship, it becomes clear that the making of statues initially served as a memorial for great figures, which gradually took on a state of sanctity and turned into their worship.(52) The word "تماثیل" (tamāthīl) is the plural of "تمثال" (timthāl), meaning lifeless statues.

52) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- Prophets are under Allah's attention even before they are commissioned. «من قبل وكُنّا به عالمین»

2- True growth (Rushd) is that which leads a person to monotheism. «رشده... اذ قال لابیه...»

3- Prophet Abraham Himself had the readiness to accept growth (Rushd). «رشده» (Rushdahu) was used, not «رشداً» (Rushdan). (The inherent talents and capacities of human beings for growth are different.)

4- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar), there is no age requirement. «قال لابیه»

5- We should start forbidding evil with our close relatives. «قال لابیه»

6- In forbidding evil, quantity is not the criterion; sometimes one person must stand against an entire group. «قال لابیه و قومه»

7- In forbidding evil, we should first address the greatest evils. «قال ما هذه التماثیل»

8- One of the successful methods in forbidding evil is addressing people's consciences. «ما هذه التماثیل»

9- We should belittle falsehoods and evils. «ما هذه التماثیل»

10- In forbidding evil, we should make people aware of their human dignity and character. «انتم لها عاكفون» (You are devoted to them) was used, not «تعكفونها» (You devote them).

11- The manifestation of growth (Rushd) lies in combating superstitions and abandoning them. «رشده اذقال ...»

12- Fathers pave the way for the deviations of their descendants. «وجدنا آبائنا»

13- The behavior of ancestors is effective in the upbringing of children, even if they do not explicitly command it. «وجدنا آبائنا»

14- Blind imitation and fanatical adherence to the behavior of ancestors are condemned and forbidden. «وجدنا آبائنا انتم و آبائكم»

15- In forbidding evil, shyness, insinuation, and formality have no place. «انتم و آبائكم فى ضلال مبین»

16- Neither antiquity nor multitude is a proof of righteousness. «انتم و آبائكم فى ضلال مبین»

17- In matters of belief, the reliance must be on reasoning, not on the path of ancestors. «وجدنا آبائنا... آبائكم فى ضلال مبین»

18- Misguidance has degrees. «ضلال مبین» (Manifest misguidance)

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ أَمْ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱللَّـٰعِبِينَ (٥٥)
They said, ‘Are you telling the truth, or are you [just] kidding?’ (55)
قَالَ بَل رَّبُّكُمْ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلَّذِى فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا۠ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (٥٦)
He said, ‘Indeed, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who originated them, and I affirm that. (56)

Key Points

The word "الحقّ" (truth/right) stands opposite the word "لاعب" (jester/playful) and means seriousness. (It asks) whether you are speaking seriously or joking.

Messages

1- For those who have been misguided generation after generation through blind imitation, accepting the truth all at once is not easy. «أَجِئْتَنَا بِالْحَقِّ أَمْ ...»

2- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and nurturing of the Creator are not exclusive to humans but encompass all existence. «رَبُّكُمْ رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ»

3- The Lord of all existence is one. «رَبُّكُمْ رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ»

(Contrary to the belief of those who assign a separate Lord for each of the heavens, the earth, and mankind.)

4- Men of God are not alone in their true belief; rather, they see themselves connected to all monotheists throughout history and to the Divine angels. «مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ»

Ayah

وَتَٱللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصْنَـٰمَكُم بَعْدَ أَن تُوَلُّوا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ (٥۷)
By Allah, I will devise a stratagem against your idols after you have gone away.’ (57)

Key Points

There is disagreement among exegetes regarding how Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) expressed this decisive and threatening statement. Some, like the authors of the Tafsirs Safi and Al-Mizan, believe that this statement was not expressed openly, and the Prophet stated it secretly, because they hold that a direct claim of fighting against the small and large gods of a nation, especially on the first day and single-handedly, is contrary to caution (prudence). However, it seems that this is not the case, and the account of the men of God is separate from the ordinary calculations of us humans, and nothing can obstruct their clear path and divine mission. Did we not read in the previous verses that Ibrahim (عليه السلام) openly told the people: «انتم وآبائكم فى ضلال مبین» (You and your fathers are in manifest error)? Did not Lady Zaynab al-Kubra (عليها السلام), while captive in Sham, tell Yazid: «انّى اَستصغِرك» (I deem you insignificant), and then shower his government with a barrage of criticism? Did not Imam Khomeini (قدس سره), at the height of the Shah's tyrannical and fully armed reign, which was supported by all internal powers and external backing, say: "The Shah must go"? And did He not tell the Shah's successor: "I will strike this government in the mouth"? And did He not say to the dominant global arrogance, America: "America cannot do a damn thing"? Truly, with which political considerations and ordinary precautions are these statements compatible? Some believe that in confronting the manifestations and agents of corruption, one should engage in passive resistance against them, just as one of the great figures regarding the cinemas during the time of the Taghut (tyrant), which were a serious means of corrupting the young generation, stated that if people stopped going to the cinema, they would automatically close down. However, the above verse condemns this way of thinking and insists that the source of corruption must be eradicated. Just as Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) burned the golden calf, and the Noble Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) destroyed Masjid al-Dirar, the base of the hypocrites.

Messages

1- Faith in the goal and certainty in the correctness of the path empower a person. «تاللّه لاكیدنّ»

2- Great deeds also require significant planning. «لاكیدنّ» The word «اكیدنّ» (I will surely plot) comes from kayd, meaning planning and designing.

3- Decisiveness is essential for successful leadership. «تاللّه لاكیدنّ» Swearing using the word «تاللّه» is stronger than swearing using the word «واللّه».(53)

4- Prophets, by connecting to the source of revelation, have the power to plan even alone. «لاكیدنّ»

5- Prophet Ibrahim's fight against the idols was completely serious and was not intended merely for intimidation. «لاكیدنّ» (The letter lām and the emphasized nūn are signs of certainty.)

6- The multitude and multiplicity of idols did not prevent Ibrahim's planning and decision. «اصنامكم»

7- If the idolaters are not present, all idols are helpless even against a single axe. «بعد أن تولّوا مدبرین» In reality, it is the idolaters who are responsible for protecting the idols, not the idols protecting them!!

8- For the execution of great goals, sometimes the presence of people is necessary. «و أن یحشرالناس ضحى» And sometimes their absence is a prerequisite. «بعد أن تولّوا مدبرین»

9- As long as there are idols, there will be a tendency toward idolatry; therefore, the idols must be destroyed. «لاكیدنّ اصنامكم»(53) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi.

Ayahs

فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذَٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرًا لَّهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ يَرْجِعُونَ (٥۸)
So he broke them into pieces—all except the biggest of them—so that they might come back to it. (58)
قَالُوا۟ مَن فَعَلَ هَـٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَآ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥۹)
They said, ‘Whoever has done this to Our gods?! He is indeed a wrongdoer!’ (59)
قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا فَتًى يَذْكُرُهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُۥٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ (٦٠)
They said, ‘We heard a young man mentioning them. He is called ‘‘Abraham.’’ ’ (60)

Key Points

On the day when all the people were leaving the city, Prophet Abraham عليه السلام accompanied them, but midway, by citing an excuse, he returned to the city and used the opportune moment to carry out his objective.

The word "جذاذ" (juthādh) means to be broken into pieces.

Although the Noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله also broke about 360 idols on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca, this act was done with the agreement of his companions and army, whereas Prophet Abraham عليه السلام crushed all those idols alone and single-handedly.

Perhaps if Prophet Abraham had also broken the largest idol, by completely wounding their feelings, he would not only have failed to achieve his goal but would have achieved the opposite result, and the opportunity for reflection would have been entirely eliminated. «الاً كبیراً لهم»

Messages

1- Idol-breaking is among the primary duties of the Divine Prophets. «فجعلهم جُذاذا»

2- If reasoning and admonition are ineffective, the time for revolutionary action arrives. «فجعلهم جذاذا»

3- Physical strength and audacity are a virtue, while weakness and humiliation are a defect. «فجعلهم جذاذا»

4- To awaken public opinion and eradicate polytheism (shirk), the destruction of certain objects and properties is necessary. (The idols of Abraham's time and the calf of Samiri had value and worth). «فجعلهم جذاذاً»

5- An idol is devoid of respect and ownership, and breaking it is permissible. «فجعلهم جذاذاً»

6- Large idols are great in the eyes of the idolaters, not in the sight of monotheists. «كبیراً لهم»

7- Breaking idols is a prelude to freeing the mind from stagnation and preparing the ground for questioning and answering. «لعلهم الیه یرجعون»

8- Deviant belief leads a person to deviant judgment. «انّه لمن الظالمین» (Sometimes a person falls so low that he considers the greatest injustice (shirk/polytheism) to be the truth, and the clearest truths (Tawhid/monotheism) to be injustice.)

9- The disbelievers first introduced Prophet Abraham عليه السلام as an oppressor so that they could easily put him on trial. «انّه لمن الظالمین»

10- Abraham’s عليه السلام cry of struggle and fight against oppression had reached everywhere. «سمعنا»

11- Young people can also create waves in the path of truth. «سمعنا فَتىً»

12- Prophet Abraham عليه السلام had prepared the ground for a revolution against the idols through his preaching before the act of destruction. «یذكرهم»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ فَأْتُوا۟ بِهِۦ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْيُنِ ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْهَدُونَ (٦۱)
They said, ‘Bring him before the people’s eyes so that they may bear witness [against him].’ (61)
قَالُوٓا۟ ءَأَنتَ فَعَلْتَ هَـٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَا يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ (٦۲)
They said, ‘Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?’ (62)
قَالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِيرُهُمْ هَـٰذَا فَسْـَٔلُوهُمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ يَنطِقُونَ (٦۳)
He said, ‘No, it was this biggest one of them who did it! Ask them, if they can speak.’ (63)

Key Points

Summoning Ibrahim (عليه السلام) before the people and requesting testimony from them was a form of propaganda and preparation for his execution. «فأتوا به على أعين النّاس لعلهم»

Question: Is this statement by Prophet Ibrahim, who said: "The great idol did this," not a lie? If it is, how should it be justified? Because Ibrahim (عليه السلام) is a Prophet and infallible (Ma'sum) and never lies.

Answer: Various views and explanations have been presented regarding the interpretation of this statement, some of which we will mention:

A: Prophet Ibrahim did not lie, because although He attributed this act to the great idol, there was a contextual clue (Qarīnah) indicating that the Prophet did not intend the statement seriously but rather wished to expose their false belief. Therefore, it is not considered a lie, as lying occurs when there is no contextual clue accompanying the speech. For this reason, if upon the arrival of a foolish person, they say: "Ibn Sina has arrived," it is not counted as a lie. (54)

B: Prophet Ibrahim used the technique known as Tawriyah (ambiguity), and His intention by "كبيرهم" (their great one) was God (Allah).

C: The Prophet did lie, but since lying is permissible in certain cases, such as reconciling disputes (Iṣlāḥ Dhāt al-Bayn), and its ruling depends on the benefit (Maṣlaḥah) or harm (Mafsadah), what greater benefit is there than the Prophet shaking the stagnant thoughts of the people with His words? (55)

D: This statement was conditional upon the idols speaking. That is, if the idols could speak, then the great idol would have done this deed. This is similar to an old man saying, "I am now a child if there is no vegetation on earth," meaning that since vegetation exists, he is not a child. (56)

54) Tafsir Namūneh. 55) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 342; Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 56) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Even idolaters rely on the issue of public testimony to confirm the crime, complete the case file, and reach a final judgment. «لعلّهم یشهدون»

2- The magnitude of Ibrahim's (عليه السلام) action was such that even the idolaters were forced to show respect. Instead of the phrase «یقال له ابراهیم» (a man called Ibrahim) in the previous Ayah, which indicated obscurity and contempt, in this Ayah, they formally addressed him by his own name, which signifies greatness and recognition. «یا ابراهیم»

3- Mockery and sarcasm against superstitions are necessary in certain situations. «بل فعله كبیرهم»

Ayahs

فَرَجَعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّكُمْ أَنتُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (٦٤)
Thereat they came to themselves and said [to one another], ‘Indeed it is you who are the wrongdoers!’ (64)
ثُمَّ نُكِسُوا۟ عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمْ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ يَنطِقُونَ (٦٥)
Then they hung their heads. [However, they said,] ‘You certainly know that they cannot speak.’ (65)

Messages

1- Awakening consciences, self-discovery, returning to oneself, and paying attention to the innate nature (Fitrah) are among the major goals of the prophets. «بل فعله كبیرهم ... فرجعوا الى انفسهم»

2- Polytheism (Shirk) is self-estrangement, and Monotheism (Tawhid) is self-discovery. «فرجعوا الى انفسهم»

3- When minds are awakened, every wrongdoer shifts the blame onto others. «انّكم انتم الظالمون» Just as on the Day of Judgment, sinners and criminals will say to one another: «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(57)

4- History, innate nature (Fitrah), and conscience are among the factors that clarify truths. «رجعوا الى انفسهم - انتم الظالمون» Those who, until yesterday, considered Abraham a wrongdoer, «مَن فَعل هذا بآلهتنا انّه لَمن الظالمین» today, by referring to their own consciences, consider themselves the wrongdoers. «اِنّكم انتم الظالمون»

57) Saba, 31.

Ayahs

قَالَ أَفَتَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَضُرُّكُمْ (٦٦)
He said, ‘Then, do you worship besides Allah that which cannot cause you any benefit or harm? (66)
أُفٍّ لَّكُمْ وَلِمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٦۷)
Fie on you and what you worship besides Allah! Do you not exercise your reason?’ (67)
قَالُوا۟ حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنصُرُوٓا۟ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ فَـٰعِلِينَ (٦۸)
They said, ‘Burn him, and help your gods, if you are to do anything!’ (68)
قُلْنَا يَـٰنَارُ كُونِى بَرْدًا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ (٦۹)
We said, ‘O fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!’ (69)
وَأَرَادُوا۟ بِهِۦ كَيْدًا فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْأَخْسَرِينَ (۷٠)
