They plotted to harm him, but We made them the biggest losers. (70)

Key Points

Men of God pursue their sacred goals under all circumstances and do not rest for a moment, even if their efforts take different forms. Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام, in the path of his divine mission, first goes to his uncle and his people and invites them toward Monotheism (Tawhid) and the worship of the One God. When no result is achieved, in the second stage, he rushes toward the idols and smashes them all. Then, he addresses their innate nature (fitrah) and begins to awaken them, and finally, after admonition and rebuke, he urges them toward contemplation and thought.

Regarding the incident of Prophet Ibrahim being thrown into the fire, many details have been mentioned in the commentaries, including that the polytheists built a catapult with the help and guidance of Satan. However, the Prophet, with his unparalleled reliance (Tawakkul) on Allah, did not seek help from any of the angels, not even Gabriel. At that time, he was engaged in supplication and intimate conversation (Munajat) with the Lord and seeking intercession (Tawassul) through Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام.

The word «أخسرین» (the greatest losers) in the Holy Quran is applied to those who commit an evil deed but imagine that their action is good. Thus, they do not realize their loss so that they might seek to compensate for it.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: When Allah commanded the fire: «كونى برداً» (Be coolness), Ibrahim’s teeth chattered from the intensity of the cold (amidst Nimrod’s fire), until it was said: «و سلاماً» (and safety/peace). (58)

58) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 318.

Messages

1- One of the methods of inviting people to religion is making them aware of the harm of irreligion. «لاینفعكم و لا یضركم»

2- The source and motivation for worship is attaining good or being saved from evil, neither of which idols are capable of. «لاینفعكم شیئاً و لا یضرّكم»

3- If a suitable ground for awakening is found, the misguided must be scorned. «اُفٍ لكم و لما تعبدون من دون اللّه»

4- Emotions must be subordinate to the doctrine. «اُفّ لكم» (Fie upon you), considering that his uncle was also an idol worshipper.

5- Idol worship is incompatible with reason. «افلا تعقلون؟» (Do you not understand/reason?) (Obstinate, foolish individuals deserve rebuke.) «أفلا تعقلون»

6- Monotheism (Tawhid) is a rational thought. «أفلا تعقلون»

7- Those who resort to force are devoid of reason and logic. «حرّقوه» (Burn him!)

8- Men of God remain steadfast in their goals until death. «حرّقوه» Burning is the most severe type of killing.

9- The survival of polytheism (Shirk) is possible through the destruction of the monotheists. «حرّقوه وانصروا آلهتكم» (Burn him and support your gods!)

10- Undue fanaticism leads man to the point of burning a Prophet. «حرّقوه و انصروا آلهتكم»

11- The enemy resorts to sacred things to incite the ignorant masses, «انصروا آلهتكم» (Support your gods!), and also appeals to zeal and fervor. «اِن كنتم فاعلین» (If you are going to act.)

12- Allah is both the Creator of means (causes) and the Nullifier of means (causes). «یا نار كونى برداً و سلاماً» (O fire, be coolness and safety!) He is the Creator of means because He makes the breath of Prophet Isa (Jesus) عليه السلام a means for reviving the dead, and He is the Nullifier of means because He removes the burning quality from the fire and makes it cool and safe for Ibrahim.

13- Existence possesses consciousness and is addressed. «یا نار كونى برداً وسلاماً» (O fire, be coolness and safety!), and He did not say: «فجعلنا النار برداً و سلاماً» (So We made the fire coolness and safety).

14- The effects of created things are subject to the divine will and decree. «یا نار كونى برداً و سلاماً»

15- The light of truth is not extinguished by the fire of disbelief. «قالوا حرّقوه... قلنا یا نار كونى برداً» (They said: Burn him!... We said: O fire, be coolness.)

16- The power of Allah can neutralize all conspiracies. «ارادوا به كیداً فجعلناهم الاخسرین» (They intended a plot against him, but We made them the greatest losers.)

17- Ultimately, the truth is victorious. «ارادوا - جعلناهم الاخسرین» (They intended [a plot] - We made them the greatest losers.)

Ayahs

وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا لِلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷۱)
We delivered him and Lot toward the land, which We have blessed for all nations. (71)
وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ نَافِلَةً ۖ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا صَـٰلِحِينَ (۷۲)
And We gave him Isaac, and Jacob as well for a grandson, and each of them We made righteous. (72)

Key Points

The enemy wanted to destroy Abraham (Ibrahim) «ارادوا به كیداً» (They intended a plot against him), but We not only saved him «نجّیناه» (We saved him), but We also granted him a blessed progeny «و وهبنا له» (and We granted him), and We made all of them among the righteous and worthy ones. «و كلاًّ جعلنا صالحین» (and We made each of them righteous). The fact that the name of Isaac (Isḥāq) عليه السلام is mentioned in this verse, while Ishmael (Ismā‘īl) عليه السلام, who is the first son of Abraham, is not mentioned, is perhaps due to drawing attention to the strange and extraordinary birth of Isaac, who was born by Divine Will from a barren mother, Sarah (Sārah), and that too in old age.

Messages

1- Allah does not abandon His saints (Awliyā’). «و نجّیناه و لوطاً» (And We saved him and Lot).

2- Whoever is not afraid of the mountain of fire and the sea of enemies will reach the blessed region. «الى الارض الّتى باركنا» (to the land which We had blessed).

3- Divine salvation depends on human effort and migration (Hijrah). «نجّیناه ... الى الارض» (We saved him... to the land).

4- Grandchildren are a Divine gift. (Jacob is Abraham’s grandson). «وهبنا له» (and We granted him).

5- Lands are not all the same. «الارض الّتى باركنا» (the land which We had blessed). Sometimes a piece of land holds blessings for all the worlds. «للعالمین» (for the worlds).

(Some attribute the blessedness of lands to the residence of Allah’s saints therein, but this verse attributes the blessing to the land itself.)

6- Blessing is in the hand of Allah. «باركنا» (We had blessed).

7- Faith and righteous deeds have repercussions and effects not only on the person himself but also on his progeny. «وهبنا له اسحاق ... كُلاّ جعلنا صالحین» (We granted him Isaac... We made each of them righteous).

8- Blessed individuals and progeny should reside in a blessed land. «الى الارض الّتى باركنا - كُلاًّ جعلنا صالحین» (to the land which We had blessed - We made each of them righteous).

9- A child is a Divine gift «وهبنا» (We granted), but what is more important than the existence of the child is his righteousness. «كُلاً جعلنا صالحین» (We made each of them righteous).

10- Righteousness is an essential condition for leadership and prophethood (Nubuwwah). «كُلاً جعلنا صالحین» (We made each of them righteous).

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِمْ فِعْلَ ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ وَإِقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِ ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ لَنَا عَـٰبِدِينَ (۷۳)
We made them imams, guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them [concerning] the performance of good deeds, the maintenance of prayers, and the giving of zakat, and they used to worship Us. (73)

Messages

1- Imamate (leadership) must be by the choice of Allah, just like prophethood (Nubuwwah). «جعلناهم ائمة»

2- Great Prophets, in addition to the station of Messengership (delivering the message of revelation), also held the responsibility of Imamate and leadership of the community. «جعلناهم ائمة»

3- If Imamate is attained through force and tyranny, it is an invitation to the Fire (Hell) «ائمّة یدعون الى النار», but if Allah grants it, it will be blessed and on the path of truth. «جعلناهم ائمّة یهدون بامرنا»

4- The guidance provided by the Prophets is not based on their own initiative or personal preference, «ما ینطق عن الهوى»(59) but rather by the command of Allah. «یهدون بامرنا»

5- Attention to performing good deeds is among the special favors of Allah. «اوحیناالیهم فعل الخیرات»

6- Prayer (Salat) and Zakat (charity) hold a special status among good deeds. «فعل الخیرات و اقام الصلاة و ایتاء الزكاة»

7- Prayer (Salat) and Zakat have been two fundamental pillars in all religions. «اقام الصلاة وایتاءالزكاة»

8- Without Prayer and Zakat, a person does not reach the degree of servitude to Allah. «اقام الصلاة و ایتاء الزكاة و كانوا لنا عابدین»

9- Divine leaders are sincere servants of Allah. «كانوا لنا عابدین»

10- The blessings of Allah should not make us heedless of servitude to Him. «وهبنا له... كانوا لنا عابدین»

(The Prophets, despite attaining the station of leadership «جعلناهم ائمة», are nevertheless still servants of Allah. «و كانوا لنا عابدین»)

11- Religion is not separate from politics. «جعلناهم ائمّة یهدون بامرنا... كانوا لنا عابدین»

12- A leader must act upon that to which he guides others. «ائمّةیهدون...كانوا لناعابدین»(59) Najm, 3.

Ayahs

وَلُوطًا ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِى كَانَت تَّعْمَلُ ٱلْخَبَـٰٓئِثَ ۗ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ فَـٰسِقِينَ (۷٤)
We gave judgement and knowledge to Lot, and We delivered him from the town which used to commit vicious acts. Indeed, they were an evil and depraved lot. (74)
وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۷٥)
And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was one of the righteous. (75)

Key Points

During the time of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham), Lot was a prophet, but the Imamate and leadership belonged to Ibrahim. Perhaps this is why, in the previous Ayah, we read concerning Isaac and Jacob: «جعلناهم ائمه» (We made them Imams), while in this Ayah, the name of Lot is mentioned separately, and the word Imams does not include him, because Ibrahim was the Imam. In this Ayah, Allah refers to four important blessings bestowed upon Prophet Lot, which are: Hukm (judgment/wisdom), Ilm (knowledge), Najat (salvation/rescue), and Dukhul fi Rahmat (entry into mercy). Perhaps the intent of Hukm (judgment) in this Ayah is the station of prophethood, just as it is used with the same meaning concerning Prophet Yahya (John): «و آتیناه الحكم صبّیاً» (And We gave him judgment [wisdom] while yet a child) (60). Allah's granting of salvation does not contradict the efforts of human beings themselves, just as the salvation of Prophet Lot عليه السلام occurred through divine command and guidance, combined with his own will and effort. «فأسر باهلك بقطع من اللیل» (So travel with your family during a portion of the night) (61). The intent of «فاسقین» (the defiantly disobedient) here is the disbelievers (kuffar), similar to the Ayah: «افمن كان مؤمناً كمن كان فاسقاً» (Is the one who is a believer like the one who is defiantly disobedient?) (62).

60) Maryam, 12. 61) Hud, 83. 62) Sajdah, 18

Messages

1- Allah saves His chosen friends (Awliya'). «نجّیناه» (We saved him).

2- Do not remain in an area prone to sin. «نجّیناه»

3- Sometimes committing one sin leads to the commission of other sins. «الخبائث» (the wicked deeds).

4- Worse than sin itself is persistence in sin and its continuation. «كانت تعمل الخبائث» (they used to commit the wicked deeds).

5- Persistence in sin is the fruit of disbelief and leads to a bad outcome. «تعمل الخبائث، انهم كانوا قوم سوء فاسقین» (they commit the wicked deeds; indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient).

6- The environment and society do not necessitate compulsion (determinism). «كانوا قوم سوء فاسقین، و ادخلناه فى رحمتنا» (they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient, and We admitted him into Our mercy).

7- If we are righteous (salih), Allah will fill us with His mercy. «ادخلناه فى رحمتنا انّه من الصالحین» (We admitted him into Our mercy; indeed, he was among the righteous).

Ayahs

وَنُوحًا إِذْ نَادَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ فَٱسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُۥ فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْكَرْبِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۷٦)
And before that Noah, when he called out, We responded to him and delivered him and his family from the great agony. (76)
وَنَصَرْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ فَأَغْرَقْنَـٰهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۷۷)
And We helped him against the people who denied Our signs. They were indeed an evil lot; so We drowned them all. (77)

Key Points

Perhaps the intent of the prayer and lamentation (the "call" - nidā) of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) is the same curse mentioned in Ayah 27 of Surah Nuh: «ربّ لا تَذر على الارض مِن الكافرین دَیّارا» (My Lord! Leave not upon the earth any dweller from among the disbelievers). O Lord! Leave no disbeliever remaining on the face of the earth. And perhaps this curse came after despairing of the people's faith, as stated: «لن یؤمن من قومك الاّ من قد آمن»(63) O Noah! None of your people will ever believe except those who have already believed. Therefore, after 950 years of preaching and so much patience and endurance, he finally raised his hands in prayer: «فدعا ربّه انّى مغلوبٌ فانتصر»(64) O God! I am overcome, so help me.

63) Hūd, 38. 64) Qamar, 10.

Messages

1- Hearing the history of others and paying attention to their calamities and difficulties makes a person patient. «و نوحاً اذ...»

2- The prayers of the Prophets are answered. «نادى - فاستجبنا» (Prayer plays a role in historical transformations.) «نادى ... اغرقنا»

3- If you do not have the power to change a corrupt society, think about saving yourself from it. «فنجینّاه ...»

4- Living in a society steeped in disbelief is a very great affliction. «الكرب العظیم»

5- Ultimately, the people of truth are victorious, and the people of falsehood are destined for destruction. «نصرناه - اغرقناهم»

6- Calamities are a reflection of the thoughts and deeds of the people themselves. «انهم كانوا قوم سوء فاغرقناهم»

Ayahs

وَدَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيْمَـٰنَ إِذْ يَحْكُمَانِ فِى ٱلْحَرْثِ إِذْ نَفَشَتْ فِيهِ غَنَمُ ٱلْقَوْمِ وَكُنَّا لِحُكْمِهِمْ شَـٰهِدِينَ (۷۸)
And [remember] David and Solomon when they gave judgement concerning the tillage when the sheep of some people strayed into it by night, and We were witness to their judgement. (78)
فَفَهَّمْنَـٰهَا سُلَيْمَـٰنَ ۚ وَكُلًّا ءَاتَيْنَا حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا ۚ وَسَخَّرْنَا مَعَ دَاوُۥدَ ٱلْجِبَالَ يُسَبِّحْنَ وَٱلطَّيْرَ ۚ وَكُنَّا فَـٰعِلِينَ (۷۹)
We gave its understanding to Solomon, and to each We gave judgement and knowledge. We disposed the mountains and the birds to glorify [Us] with David, and We have been the doer [of these things]. (79)

Key Points

What is gathered from the narrations and commentaries regarding the incident mentioned in this Ayah is that: Sheep entered a person's vineyard at night and damaged and ruined it. The owner of the grapevines brought a complaint before David (Dawood) عليه السلام, and the Prophet ruled that all the sheep should be handed over to the owner of the garden to compensate for the loss. However, his son, Solomon (Sulayman), suggested another solution to his father: that the sheep be placed at the disposal of the plaintiff so that he could benefit from their yield, and the garden be handed over to the owner of the sheep so that he could repair it. After the compensation was complete, each party would take back their original property. (65) It is clear that both Prophets were seeking a way to compensate for the loss, but the father (David عليه السلام) saw it in handing over the sheep, while the son (Solomon عليه السلام) wanted to provide compensation through the benefits derived from the sheep. Allah says: Although We granted both of them knowledge and wisdom, in this specific case, Solomon's judgment was better. A hadith states that whenever David عليه السلام began reciting the heavenly book, the "Zabur" (Psalms), there was no mountain, stone, or bird that did not join him in harmony upon hearing his voice. (66)

65) Al-Faqih, Vol. 3, p. 57. 66) Tafasir Safi and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Respect for the father must be observed, even in the order in which his name is mentioned alongside his son. «داود و سلیمان»

2- Judgment (Qada') is one of the functions of the Prophets. «اذ یحكمان»

3- The Prophets are the primary and true authorities for resolving people's disputes. «اذ یحكمان»

4- Compensation for damage and loss is not limited to intentional cases. «نفشت فیه غنم القوم»

5- The judge must consider Allah as a Witness and Observer over his work. «كنّا لحكمهم شاهدین»

6- Correct understanding and comprehension are not possible without Divine favor. «فهّمناها»

7- Sometimes Allah grants a blessing to the son that He does not grant to the father. «فهّمناهاسلیمان»

8- The glorification (Tasbih) of the mountains is not merely an echo of the glorifier's voice, but is itself a real and true glorification. «مع داود یسبّحن»

9- All creatures, including mountains and birds, possess a degree of consciousness and are engaged in the glorification of Allah. «یسبّحن»

Ayahs

وَعَلَّمْنَـٰهُ صَنْعَةَ لَبُوسٍ لَّكُمْ لِتُحْصِنَكُم مِّنۢ بَأْسِكُمْ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُمْ شَـٰكِرُونَ (۸٠)
We taught him the making of coats of mail for you, to protect you from your [own] violence. Will you then be grateful? (80)
وَلِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ عَاصِفَةً تَجْرِى بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا ۚ وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَـٰلِمِينَ (۸۱)
And [We disposed] for Solomon the tempestuous wind which blew by his command toward the land which We have blessed, and We have knowledge of all things. (81)

Key Points

| Although in this Ayah, a strong wind is mentioned which blew in the blessed land (Syria/Levant), yet, as is understood from Ayah 36 of Surah Sad, Prophet Sulayman also had the power of control and command over gentle winds in other regions. «سخّرنا له الریح تجرى بامره رخاءً حیث اصاب» We placed the wind at Sulayman's disposal so that it would go gently and slowly wherever he intended. Regarding what distance Prophet Sulayman عليه السلام would cover with the wind and in what time, in Ayah 12 of Surah Saba' we read: «و لسلیمان الرّیح غُدوّها شهر و رواحها شهر» And for Sulayman was (movement by) the wind, its morning journey (equal to the distance of) one month, and its evening journey (also equal to the travel of) one month.

Messages

1- Inventions are Divine inspirations. «علّمناه»

2- Prophet Dawud (David) is the innovator of the armor-making industry. «علّمناه صنعة لبوس»

3- Prophets were also people of work, skill, and profession. «صنعة لبوس»

4- Prophets protected people through natural and ordinary human means. «صنعة لبوس»

5- Industry must be in the service of the people, not under the domination of the arrogant and specific individuals. «لتحصنكم»

6- Preparing the prerequisites for defensive matters is necessary and essential. «لتحصنكم»

7- The blessing of security deserves gratitude. «لتحصنكم ... شاكرون» (Security is in the shadow of industry.) «صنعة لبوس لتحصنكم»

8- Everyone (the innovator and the people) must thank Allah for benefiting from inventions and industries. «شاكرون»

9- The friends (Awliya') of Allah can, by Divine permission, exercise control over nature. «تجرى بامره»

10- Winds also possess consciousness and understand and execute the command of Allah and His Awliya'. «تجرى بامره»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينِ مَن يَغُوصُونَ لَهُۥ وَيَعْمَلُونَ عَمَلًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَكُنَّا لَهُمْ حَـٰفِظِينَ (۸۲)
Among the devils were some who dived for him and performed tasks other than that, and We were watchful over them. (82)

Key Points

The devils (Shayatin) are from the jinn species, conscious beings who, like humans, have religious obligations (taklif). Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), in addition to governing humans, also governed some of them. «وحُشِر لسلیمان جنوده من الجنّ والانس»(67) Diving (ghawwasi) was among the activities the jinn performed for Sulayman. «یغوصون له» However, the skill of the jinn was not limited only to diving. «یعملون عملاً دون ذلك» Other verses refer to some of their other works. «یعملون له ما یشاء من محاریب وتماثیل و جفان كالجواب و قدور راسیات»(68) They built for him whatever he desired, including high and beautiful sanctuaries (mihrabs) and temples, and various household items such as large pots and basins.

67) An-Naml, 17. 68) Saba', 11.

Messages

1- Art and craftsmanship also exist among the jinn. «من الشیاطین ...»

2- Jinn can be in the service of mankind. «یغوصون له»

3- If there is no control and protection, corruption can find its way in, even in the unique government of Sulayman. «كنّا لهم حافظین»

Ayahs

۞ وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَسَّنِىَ ٱلضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (۸۳)
And [remember] Job, when he called out to his Lord, ‘Indeed distress has befallen me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful.’ (83)
فَٱسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُۥ فَكَشَفْنَا مَا بِهِۦ مِن ضُرٍّ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ أَهْلَهُۥ وَمِثْلَهُم مَّعَهُمْ رَحْمَةً مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْعَـٰبِدِينَ (۸٤)
So We answered his prayer and removed his distress, and We gave him [back] his family along with others like them, as a mercy from Us and an admonition for the devout. (84)

Key Points

Prophet Ayyub (Job) is a prophet who has been honored in the Holy Quran with the badge of patience. «اِنّا وجدناه صابراً، نعم العبد، اِنّه أوّاب»(69) Indeed, We found him patient. What an excellent servant! Indeed, he was ever-turning (to Allah). Although Prophet Ayyub was afflicted with very severe difficulties and illnesses, contrary to what is mentioned in some books, these issues did not cause people to be repulsed by him. This is because, in that case, besides being contrary to the principle of Lutf (Divine Grace), it is also inconsistent with many narrations and commentaries, including Tafsir al-Mizan, Fakhr Razi, Atyab al-Bayan, Abu al-Futuh, and Namuneh.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the phrase «و مِثلهم مَعهم» (and the like of them with them)—how did He give Ayyub the like of them as well? The Imam said: Allah returned to him those of his children who had previously died a natural death, along with those who had perished (in the affliction).(70)

69) Sad, 44. 70) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 253.

Messages

1- Recalling and reviewing the history of the lives of great figures is the best means of solace, patience, guidance, and direction. «و ایّوب»

2- Prophets, too, in their material lives, were afflicted with difficulties and illnesses just like other ordinary people. «مَسّنى الضُر»

3- Although afflictions appear outwardly as harm, in many instances, they are inwardly a hidden grace (Lutf-e Khafi) and Divine Mercy. «مَسّنى الضُرّ و أنت أرحم الرّاحمین»

4- Calamities and misfortunes do not always have a punitive or disciplinary aspect; rather, sometimes they are for the bestowal of rank and have a testing aspect. «مَسّنى الضُر» (Considering that the Prophets are infallible, hardships are not due to committing sin or punishment.)

5- The Prophets are models of etiquette. «مسّنى الضُرّ و أنت أرحم الرّاحمین» Prophet Ayyub عليه السلام did not say: O Lord! Remove the harm from me, but rather prayed in a manner that implied: O God, You Yourself know (my condition).

6- When supplicating, we should call upon Allah using an attribute that is appropriate to our need. «ارحم الرّاحمین»

7- The supplication of the Prophets is answered. «فاستجبنا»

8- Divine favor is greater than man's request and supplication. «كشفنا ما به من ضرّ و آتیناه ...» (Prophet Ayyub عليه السلام requested the "removal of harm" from Allah, but the Lord, in addition to answering his request, returned his family to him.)

9- Children are a Divine favor and mercy. «آتیناه اهله و مثلهم معهم»

10- Allah is the possessor of both Generosity (Jūd) and Might (Jabarūt). «آتیناه اهله و مثلهم معهم» In the Qunūt (supplication) of the Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Qurban prayers, we recite: «اللهم ... اهل الجود والجبروت ...» Here, Allah returned everything that had been taken from Ayyub and, moreover, doubled it. He both compensated for his past and showed generosity and kindness toward his future.

11- There is a special connection between supplication on the one hand, and Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and the outpouring of Divine Mercy on the other. «نادى , ربّه, رحمة»

12- The answering of the Prophets' prayers is not based on their personal merit, but rather on the basis of Divine Grace and Mercy. «رحمة من عندنا»

13- Calamities have various dimensions: sometimes they are for testing, sometimes for intellectual and scientific growth, and sometimes for providing a lesson to others. «ذكرى للعابدین»

14- Worship is the ground for accepting admonition. «ذكرى للعابدین»

15- All people, even the devout worshipers, need reminder. «ذكرى للعابدین»

Ayahs

وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۸٥)
And [remember] Ishmael, Idris, and Dhul-Kifl—each of them was among the patient. (85)
وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۸٦)
We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were among the righteous. (86)

Key Points

Isma'il (عليه السلام) was the blessed son of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) and the noble ancestor of the esteemed Messenger of Islam. He displayed the most beautiful epic of obedience to the command of the Truth (Allah) and patience regarding the Divine instruction in the incident of the truthful dream where his father was commanded to slaughter him. Prophet Idris (عليه السلام) was the ancestor of Prophet Nuh (عليه السلام) and the first person who engaged in the science of astronomy, taught others how to sew clothes, and wrote script with a pen, engaging in writing. It is said that thirty scriptures (Suhuf) were revealed to him, and finally, he ascended to the heavens and is still alive now. (71) "و رفعناه مكاناً علیاً" (72) Prophet Dhul-Kifl (عليه السلام) is one of the Divine Prophets who lived after Prophet Sulayman (عليه السلام) and before Prophet Isa (عليه السلام). (73) As is well-known, his grave is located on the route from Karbala to Najaf. We read in Tafsir al-Tibyan: Because this Divine Prophet had pledged to fast during the days and dedicate the nights to worship, to judge only based on truth, and not to become angry except for the pleasure of Allah, and remained faithful until the end to what he had undertaken and committed to, he was named "Dhul-Kifl," meaning "the one with the commitment." Some, like Fakhr Razi, believe that "Dhul-Kifl" is the title of Prophet Ilyas (Elias) and that he was famous by this name and was one of the righteous. However, considering a narration cited in Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan from Abdul Azim al-Hasani from Imam Jawad (عليه السلام), which states that Allah sent one hundred and twenty-four thousand Prophets for the guidance of humanity, of whom three hundred and thirteen were Messengers (Mursalin), and Dhul-Kifl is one of those Messengers, we consider Dhul-Kifl not to be the title of Prophet Ilyas, but rather one of the Prophets and Messengers. Prophet Isma'il (عليه السلام) showed patience in the face of the Divine command for his father, Prophet Ibrahim, to sacrifice him, and Prophet Idris also invited people to the true religion for 365 years, but no one believed in him. (74)

71) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan. 72) Maryam, 57. 73) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan. 74) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. Let us keep the names of the great alive and recount their perfections. "و اسماعیل و ..."
  2. Patience is a valuable trait that Allah praises in those who possess it. "كلّ من الصّابرین"
  3. Patience is the groundwork for man's entry into Divine Mercy. "كلٌّ من ّلصابرین و ادخلناهم فى رحمتنا"
  4. Attaining ranks and stations follows success in Divine trials. "الصابرین و ادخلناهم"
  5. Benefiting from Divine Mercy depends on righteous deeds. "ادخلناهم - من الصالحین"
  6. Patience (Sabr) and righteousness (Salah) are characteristics of the Prophets, and whoever is described by them bears the mark of the Prophets. "و اسماعیل و ... من الصابرین - من الصالحین"

Ayahs

وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَـٰضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۸۷)
And [remember] the Man of the Fish, when he left in a rage, thinking that We would not put him to hardship. Then he cried out in the darkness, ‘There is no god except You! You are immaculate! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers!’ (87)
فَٱسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُۥ وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلْغَمِّ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُـۨجِى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۸۸)
So We answered his prayer and delivered him from the agony; and thus do We deliver the faithful. (88)

Key Points

What has been mentioned in the commentaries regarding the story of Prophet Yunus (Jonah) is that he invited people to monotheism (worship of Allah) for many years, but only two people believed in him: one was a devout worshipper ('abid) and the other was a knowledgeable person ('alim). When the situation continued in this manner, the worshipper suggested to Yunus عليه السلام that he curse them. The Prophet, who was exasperated by their stubbornness and obstinacy, proceeded with this action, and as soon as the signs of the calamity appeared, without seeking permission from Allah, he left the area in anger and wrath, thinking that he was finally rid of them. However, he did not know that Allah would make life difficult for him because of this action. When he left that place, he went towards the sea, boarded a ship, and set off for another region. Midway through the journey, the ship suddenly became distressed upon encountering a large whale, and the people on board were close to drowning. The ship owners decided that, to avert this calamity, they should throw one person from the ship into the sea to save themselves from this disaster. When they drew lots, the name of Yunus عليه السلام came up, so they threw him into the sea. Immediately, the whale swallowed him and went deep into the sea, but by Divine command, it was prevented from eating or digesting him. In the darkness of the fish's belly and the depths of the water, Yunus عليه السلام realized his wrongdoing—his inappropriate departure from among his people—and confessed to it. Then Allah answered his prayer and saved him from that state. Thereafter, Yunus received the title "Dhul-Nun" (ذوالنون), meaning "the Companion of the Fish." It is stated in Ayahs 143 and 144 of Surah As-Saffat: «فلولا انّه كان من المسبّحین . للبث فى بطنه الى یوم یبعثون» meaning: If it had not been that he was counted among those who glorify (Allah), he would certainly have remained confined in its belly until the Day they are resurrected.

Messages

1- When recounting history, let us not always speak of successes and sweet moments, but also refer to bitter events and failures. «وذاالنون»

2- Never consider your Divine mission finished or abandon it. «اذ ذهب»

3- Sometimes a hasty, thoughtless, and unauthorized action brings severe punishment. «اذ ذهب - فنادى فى الظلمات»

4- Allah is aware of our imaginations, thoughts, and assumptions. «فظن ان لن نقدر علیه»

5- Sometimes the Prophets are unaware of their own future events. «فظن ان لن نقدر علیه»

6- Sometimes an inappropriate fit of anger afflicts a Prophet of Allah. «مغاضباً - فنادى »

7- Sometimes the unworthiness of an action leads to various kinds of darkness. «الظلمات»

8- When analyzing afflictions, we must declare Allah free from imperfection (Tanzih) and seek the source of the affliction in our own actions. «سبحانك اِنّى كنت»

9- If movements, loves, and hatreds are not by the command of Allah and pleasing to Him, they will not yield the desired result. «ذَهَب مغاضباً - اِنّى كنت من الظالمین»

10- Confession of sin before Allah is itself a perfection and one of the manners of supplication (Du'a). «اِنّى كنت من الظالمین»

11- A supplication that includes the affirmation of Monotheism (Tawhid), the glorification of the Lord (Tanzih), and the confession of fault, mistake, and sin, is answered. «سبحانك اِنّى كنتُ ... فاستجبنا»

12- The glorification of Allah (Tanzih) and the confession of sin are the secret to salvation from calamities and deprivations. «سبحانك اِنّى كنتُ ... فاستجبنا»

13- The only path to salvation is the will and decree of Allah. «فاستجینا له و نجّیناه»

14- The stories of the Quran are not merely finished events; they are an ongoing process and a permanent tradition (Sunnah). «كذلك»

15- The salvation of the believers is a tradition and law of Allah. «كذلك ننجى المؤمنین»

16- Whoever recites this invocation in that state will be saved. «اِنّى كنتُ من الظالمین فاستجبنا... كذلك ننجى المؤمنین»

Ayahs

وَزَكَرِيَّآ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْنِى فَرْدًا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْوَٰرِثِينَ (۸۹)
And [remember] Zechariah, when he cried out to his Lord, ‘My Lord! Do not leave me without an heir, and You are the best of inheritors.’ (89)
فَٱسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُۥ وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥ يَحْيَىٰ وَأَصْلَحْنَا لَهُۥ زَوْجَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ وَيَدْعُونَنَا رَغَبًا وَرَهَبًا ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ لَنَا خَـٰشِعِينَ (۹٠)
So We answered his prayer, and gave him John, and cured for him his wife [of infertility]. Indeed, they were active in [performing] good works, and they would supplicate Us with eagerness and awe and were humble before Us. (90)

Key Points

Whenever the Prophets faced any difficulty, they hastened to Allah and sought its resolution from their Lord. We encountered examples of these requests in the previous verses.(75) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, during the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), supplicated to Allah, saying: O God! You took Ubaydah ibn al-Harith from me on the Day of Badr, and Hamzah on the Day of Uhud, and this is Ali. He then recited this verse: «ربّ لا تذرنى فرداً و انت خیر الوارثین»(76) Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said regarding «رغباً و رهباً» (desire and awe): Desire (Raghban) is when you place the palm of your hand towards the sky during supplication, and awe (Rahban) is when you turn the back of your hands towards the sky.(77)

75) We read the supplications of Prophets Noah, Job, and Jonah in the previous verses. 76) Bihar, Vol. 38, p. 309. 77) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 497.

Messages

1- Never despair of Divine Mercy. «و زكریا اذ نادى » (Allah grants a child even to an elderly man like Zachariah علیه السلام from a barren wife.)

2- Mentioning the word «ربّ» (Lord) and repeating it in supplication makes requests more likely to be answered. «نادى ربّه ربّ لا تذرنى»

3- When supplicating, we should remember Allah by the attribute that is most relevant to our request. «لا تذرنى فرداً و انت خیرالوارثین»

4- Belief in Allah being the «خیرالوارثین» (Best of Inheritors) does not contradict natural desires, such as the love for a child and requesting it from the Lord. «لا تذرنى فرداً و انت خیرالوارثین»

5- The supplication of the Prophets is answered. «فاستجبنا»

6- A child is a Divine gift. «و وهبنا له یحیى »

7- Allah has special regard for His saints. The word «له» (for him) is repeated three times. «فاستجبنا له - و وهبنا له - اصلحنا له»

8- Allah’s Hand is not tied, and He can make even a barren woman fit for conception. «اصلحنا له زوجه»

9- Man can rectify barrenness through supplication. «فاستجبناله ووهبنا له»

10- Complete happiness is achieved when all family members are partners, like-minded, and synchronized in performing good deeds. «انهم كانوا یسارعون فى الخیرات»

11- What increases the value of good deeds is speed and enthusiasm in performing them. «یسارعون»

12- Fear and hope regarding Allah (Khawf and Raja) are two valuable elements, even for the Prophets. «رغباً و رهباً»

13- When supplicating, one must call upon God with a state of fear and hope. «یدعوننا رغباً و رهباً»

14- The saints of Allah bow their heads only before Him. «لنا خاشعین» (to Us humbly) and not «خاشعین لنا» (humbly to Us).

15- Human perfections are valuable when they are permanent, not fleeting or temporary. «كانوا یسارعون، یدعوننا، و كانوا لنا خاشعین»

16- Speed in good deeds and supplication accompanied by fear and hope are effective in the answering of prayers. «فاستجبنا له ... كانوا یسارعون فى الخیرات و یدعوننا ...»

Ayah

وَٱلَّتِىٓ أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَهَا فَنَفَخْنَا فِيهَا مِن رُّوحِنَا وَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا وَٱبْنَهَآ ءَايَةً لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹۱)
And [remember] her who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her Our spirit, and made her and her son a sign for all the nations. (91)

Messages

1- The focus in presenting Quranic content is the offering of human models and perfections, and there is no difference between men and women in this regard. «الّتى اَحصنت فرجها»

2- Divine favors descend when a capacity (receptivity) exists. «احصنت فرجها، فنفخنا»

3- Chastity is among the most prominent perfections of a woman. «احصنت فرجها»

4- The existence of a single perfection in a person brings many blessings. «احصنت فرجها، فنفخنا، روحنا، جعلناها، آیة للعالمین»

5- The chastity of a mother can elevate her and her children to the highest ranks. «وجعلناها و ابنها آیة للعالمین»

6- The status of a woman reaches the point where Allah praises her like the prophets and makes her His sign and token among the worlds. «آیة للعالمین»

Ayahs

إِنَّ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً وَأَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُونِ (۹۲)
Indeed this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord. So worship Me. (92)
وَتَقَطَّعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ كُلٌّ إِلَيْنَا رَٰجِعُونَ (۹۳)
They have fragmented their religion among themselves, but everyone of them will return to Us. (93)

Messages

1- All Divine religions are unified in their destination and goal. «ان هذه امّتكم امّة واحدة»

2- Monotheism (Tawhid) and unity of word (Wahdat al-Kalimah) are the foundation of the Divine religions. «اُمّة واحدة»

3- Lordship (Rububiyyah) necessitates worship. «و انّا ربّكم فاعبدون»

4- The source of division in the Divine religions and the religion of Islam is the people themselves. «تقطّعوا امرهم»

5- Those who create division must prepare themselves for accountability in the Divine presence. «وتقطعوا امرهم، كل الینا راجعون»

Ayah

فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَا كُفْرَانَ لِسَعْيِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ كَـٰتِبُونَ (۹٤)
Whoever is faithful and does righteous deeds, his endeavour shall not go unappreciated, and We will indeed record it for him. (94)

Key Points

The promise of reward (pādāsh), recompense (ajr), and merit (thawāb) has been referred to in the Quran using various expressions. In one place, He says: We do not waste the reward of the good-doers, «اِنّا لا نضیع أجرالمحسنین»(78) In another place, He says: The effort and striving of people are appreciated and thanked, «كان سعیهم مشكورا»(79) and Allah is Appreciative (Shākir) and Most Appreciative (Shakūr), «اِنّ اللّه شاكر علیم» In yet another place, He says: Allah does not diminish any part of your reward, even a particle. «لا یلتكم من اعمالكم شیئاً»(80) And if you do an atom's weight of good, you will see it, «فمَن یعمل مثقال ذرّةٍ خیراً یَرَه»(81).

78) Yusuf, 56. 79) Isra, 19. 80) Hujurat, 14. 81) Zilzal, 7.

Messages

1- Maintaining unity is among the righteous deeds. «تقطعوا امرهم ... فمن یعمل ...»

2- Islam is the school of standards and values, and it praises every value from every person and at every time. «مَن یعمل من الصالحات»

3- If you do not achieve success in all good deeds, at least perform whatever amount of them is possible for you. «یعمل من الصالحات» (performs some righteous deeds), not «یعمل الصالحات» (performs all righteous deeds).

4- Divine motivations and goals give value to actions. «و هو مؤمن»

5- In the divine worldview, no one's positive effort remains without response or result. «فلاكفران» (We, too, should not overlook people's good deeds).

6- What is important is the striving and effort to perform a good deed, even if it does not fully materialize. «لسعیه»

7- Human deeds are recorded, and the reward is based on calculation and reckoning. «كاتبون»

Ayahs

وَحَرَٰمٌ عَلَىٰ قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهَآ أَنَّهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ (۹٥)
It is forbidden for [the people of] any town that We have destroyed [to return to the world]: they shall not return, (95)
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فُتِحَتْ يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ وَهُم مِّن كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنسِلُونَ (۹٦)
until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they race down from every slope, (96)

Key Points

Regarding what is meant by the non-return of the disbelievers, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: For individuals who have been destroyed (by Divine wrath) in this world, worldly raj'ah (return) is no longer possible, but for disbelievers who have not been destroyed (and have died a natural death), this return to the world is possible. (82) Therefore, the intention is non-return to this world, and it is obvious that in the Hereafter, everyone will return.

As mentioned in verses 93 to 98 of Surah Al-Kahf, Gog and Magog (Yajuj and Majuj) were tribes who, through their attacks on neighboring regions, caused corruption on earth, destruction, and ruin, until a barrier was built by Dhul-Qarnayn, and their path of infiltration into other regions was blocked. But one of the signs of the Day of Judgment and the end of the world's lifespan is the destruction of this barrier and the renewed invasion of these tribes. As He said there: «فاذا جاء وعد ربّى جعله دكّاء» (83) and here, in the next verse, He says: «واقترب الوعد الحقّ». In any case, these verses are a type of prophecy regarding the invasion of aggressors and their pouring forth into all regions of the earth.

82) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan and Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 24. 83) Kahf, 98.

Messages

1- The raj'ah (return) and the wish for the return of the destroyed nations will only be realized at the time of the appearance of Gog and Magog. «لا یرجعون حتّى ...»

2- The world will become the arena for the rebellious. «حتّى اذا فتحت»

3- In the future of history, corruptors will also rampage. «من كلّ حدب»

Ayah

وَٱقْتَرَبَ ٱلْوَعْدُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِىَ شَـٰخِصَةٌ أَبْصَـٰرُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَا قَدْ كُنَّا فِى غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا بَلْ كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۹۷)
and the true promise draws near [to its fulfillment], behold, the faithless will look on with a fixed gaze: ‘Woe to us! We have certainly been oblivious of this! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers!’ (97)

Key Points

The word shākhiṣah (شاخصة) is derived from the root shukhūṣ (شخوص), meaning to move out from one place toward another. Since the eye, in a state of astonishment, takes on a condition as if it wants to pop out of its socket, this state of the eye is also called shukhūṣ. Although the Hour (Qiyāmah) seems distant, in reality, it is near. "اقترب الوعد" (The Promise has drawn near). This nearness is also emphasized in other verses, including the verse "اقترب الساعة..." (84) and "انّهم یرونه بعیداً و نراه قریباً" (85).

84) Al-Qamar, 1. 85) Al-Ma'ārij, 7-8.

Messages

1- The invasion of the mischief-makers (Gog and Magog) is a sign of the end of the world's lifespan and the nearness of the Hour. "حتّى اذا فتحت یأجوج ... و اقترب الوعد الحقّ"

2- The occurrence of the Hour is certain and inevitable. "اِقترب الوعد الحقّ"

3- The Hour and its events are sudden. "فاذاهى"

4- The Hour is a day of shock and bewilderment. "شاخصة ابصار الّذین"

5- The Hour is a day when dormant consciences awaken and express regret. "یا ویلنا"

6- Negligence regarding the Hour is one of the clear signs of disbelief and disbelievers. "كفروا ... كنّا فى غفلة"

7- The root of all deviations and misfortunes is negligence. "كنّا فى غفلة"

8- Negligence regarding the Hour leads to oppression against oneself and others. "كنّا فى غفلة... كنّا ظالمین"

9- The Hour is a day of acknowledgment and confession. "كنّا ظالمین"

Ayah

إِنَّكُمْ وَمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ أَنتُمْ لَهَا وَٰرِدُونَ (۹۸)
Indeed you and what [idols] you worship besides Allah shall be fuel for hell, and you will enter it. (98)

Key Points

| Question: This verse states that both the idolaters and their objects of worship will be the fuel and kindling of Hell. Do individuals like Prophet Jesus عليه السلام, who have been taken as objects of worship, fall under this rule, or are they exempt? Answer: These types of objects of worship are exempted, because, firstly, the Quran refers to those objects of worship using the phrase "وما تعبدون" (what you worship), and the presence of the word "ما" (ma) in this expression refers to non-sentient beings,(86) secondly, the addressees of this verse were the idolaters of Mecca who worshipped idols made of stone, wood, and so forth, thirdly, it is mentioned in the subsequent verses of this very Surah that: those who (like حضرت عیسى) have been given a good promise by Allah will be kept far away from Hell.

86) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, in response to the above question, said: It is the custom of the Arabs to use the word "ما" (ma) for those without intellect, and "ما تعبدون" (what you worship) refers to idols made of stone and wood. (Bihar, Vol. 9, p. 282).

Messages

1- Worshipping other than Allah leads both the worshipper and the worshipped object to Hell. «اِنّكم و ماتعبدون... حصب جهنّم»

2- The fuel of Hell is the disbelievers and their imaginary objects of worship. «اِنّكم و ماتعبدون... حصب جهنّم»

Ayahs

لَوْ كَانَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ءَالِهَةً مَّا وَرَدُوهَا ۖ وَكُلٌّ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۹۹)
Had they been gods, they would not have entered it, and they will all remain in it [forever]. (99)
لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌ وَهُمْ فِيهَا لَا يَسْمَعُونَ (۱٠٠)
Their lot therein will be groaning, and they will not hear anything in it. (100)

Key Points

Usually, the expression of sounds in the Arabic language follows the pattern fa'īl, such as: zafīr (sigh/groan), shahīq (inhalation/gasp), za'īr (roar), and harīr (growl). Zafīr refers to a sound accompanied by exhalation. The intent of the phrase "لا یسمعون" (they shall not hear) is not that the inhabitants of Hell hear no sound at all, but rather that whatever they cry out, they receive no saving response, because there are verses in the Qur'an that indicate the hearing of the people of Hell, such as when they ask the officer of the torment for respite, or when they request salvation from Allah, or when they answer when questioned.

Messages

1- We should not attach our hearts to anything other than Allah, nor tie our destiny to false and helpless gods. «لوكان ... ما وردوها»

2- Helplessness and condemnation are incompatible with the status of Divinity. «لوكان ... ما وردوها»

3- The punishment for polytheism (shirk) is an eternal punishment. «كل فیها خالدون»

4- Hell is the abode of unanswered groans. «فیها زفیر ... لا یسمعون»

5- The lack of response to their groans and cries is itself another torment for the inhabitants of Hell. «و هم فیها لا یسمعون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُم مِّنَّا ٱلْحُسْنَىٰٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَنْهَا مُبْعَدُونَ (۱٠۱)
Indeed those to whom there has gone beforehand [the promise of] the best reward from Us will be kept away from it. (101)

Key Points

| It is narrated in traditions that a group asked the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: If the Quran has designated the abode of the worshipper and the worshipped (other than Allah) as the Fire of Hell (87), then what is the status of Jesus عليه السلام and Mary عليها السلام, who were unintentionally worshipped by others? The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله recited this noble Ayah in response to them.

87) Al-Anbiya, 98.

Messages

1- Allah fulfills the promises He has made to the people of faith and piety. «سبقت لهم مناالحُسنى اولئك عنها مبعدون»

2- Whoever enters Paradise will not enter Hell again. «اولئك عنها مبعدون»

Ayah

لَا يَسْمَعُونَ حَسِيسَهَا ۖ وَهُمْ فِى مَا ٱشْتَهَتْ أَنفُسُهُمْ خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱٠۲)
They will not hear even its faint sound and they will remain [forever] in what their souls desire. (102)

Key Points

Even if a person in this world lives in comfort and prosperity, willingly or unwillingly, unpleasant news and bitter events may reach his ears, diminishing his pleasure and enjoyment. However, in the Hereafter, the inhabitants of Paradise, far removed from any bad news, harsh noise, or the wailing and cries of the afflicted, will be engaged in complete enjoyment and fulfillment. Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام states in Sermon 183 of Nahj al-Balaghah: Allah has honored the inhabitants of Paradise so greatly that their ears will not even hear the sound of the Hellfire and the wailing and cries of its inhabitants.(88)

The Quran contains various verses describing the quantitative and qualitative breadth of the blessings of Paradise. In one verse, He says: «ما تشتهیه الانفس و تلذّ الاعین»(89) (Whatever the soul desires and whatever the eyes delight in). In this verse, He says: «و هم فى ما اشتهت انفسهم خالدون» (They will abide eternally in whatever their souls desire), and they will be blessed forever in whatever their hearts desire. And elsewhere, He describes it with this expression: «فلا تعلم نفس ما اخفى لهم»(90) (No soul knows what has been hidden for them) — no one knows what pleasures and fulfillments have been stored and hidden for the inhabitants of Paradise. In a Hadith from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, it is stated that: The quality of the blessings of Paradise has neither been heard by an ear nor seen by an eye.(91)

88) Tafsir Namuneh. 89) Al-Zukhruf, 71. 90) Al-Sajdah, 17. 91) Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 295.

Messages

1- In the Hereafter, the inhabitants of Paradise will be engaged in fulfillment, free from the suffering and wailing of the afflicted. «لایسمعون حسیسها»

2- The true satisfaction of instincts is only possible in Paradise. «فیما اشتهت»

3- In Paradise, there is neither limitation in blessings «فیما اشتهت» nor limitation in the duration of enjoyment. «خالدون»

Ayah

لَا يَحْزُنُهُمُ ٱلْفَزَعُ ٱلْأَكْبَرُ وَتَتَلَقَّىٰهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ هَـٰذَا يَوْمُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ (۱٠۳)
The Great Terror will not upset them, and the angels will receive them [saying]: ‘This is your day which you were promised.’ (103)

Key Points

Based on narrations, the addressees of the verse are those who, in addition to possessing correct beliefs, follow the path of leaders who have been appointed by Allah and His Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله. (92)

92) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Warding off evil is more important than attracting benefit. Therefore, the discussion first mentions being far from sorrows, and then receiving glad tidings. «لایحزنهم الفزع الاكبر و تتلقاهم الملائكه»

2- The hardships of this world, no matter how great they may be, are insignificant compared to the calamities of the Hereafter. «الفزع الاكبر»

3- On the Day of Judgment, the angels will speak with humans. «تتلقّاهم الملائكه»

4- Welcoming and meeting the believers is an angelic task. «تتلقّاهم الملائكه»

5- If the world today is to the liking of the tyrants (Taghut), let us not grieve, for the time of the believers will also arrive. «هذا یومكم»

6- Divine promises are certain. «كنتم توعدون»

Ayah

يَوْمَ نَطْوِى ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَطَىِّ ٱلسِّجِلِّ لِلْكُتُبِ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأْنَآ أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍ نُّعِيدُهُۥ ۚ وَعْدًا عَلَيْنَآ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا فَـٰعِلِينَ (۱٠٤)
The day We shall roll up the heaven, like rolling of the scrolls [meant] for writings. We will bring it back just as We began the first creation—a promise [binding] on Us. [That] indeed We will do. (104)

Key Points

The word sijill (سجلّ) originally referred to a piece of stone upon which they would write, but gradually it came to be applied to the sheets upon which contents are written. The similarity between the advent of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) and the initial creation of the world has been pointed out repeatedly in the Quran. In one place, He says: «كما بدئكم تعودون» (93) (Just as He originated you, you will return). In another Ayah, we read: «هوالّذى یبدء الخلق ثمّ یعیده و هو اهون علیه» (94) (He is the One Who originates the creation, then repeats it, and that is easier for Him). The intent of 'the heaven' (السماء) in this Ayah is all the heavens, because in another place He has said: «و السموات مطویّات بیمینه ...» (95) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Man will be resurrected naked on the Day of Judgment. Then His Holiness recited this Ayah: «كما بدءنا اوّل خلق...» (96)

93) Al-A'raf, 26. 94) Ar-Rum, 27. 95) Az-Zumar, 67. 96) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 111.

Messages

1- The cosmic order is neither eternal nor everlasting, and eventually, it will undergo change and transformation. «یوم نطوى السماء... كما بَدَءنا»

2- The power of Allah regarding the folding up of the heavens is as easy as folding a scroll. «كطىّ السجلّ»

3- Analogy (or similitude) is the best and most eloquent way for comprehension and understanding. «كطىّ السجلّ»

4- Just as the folding of a scroll does not mean the erasure of its writings, the folding up of the heavens is not a sign of the annihilation of creation. «كطىّ السجلّ للكتب»

5- Any change and transformation in the heavens occurs by the Will and Decree of Allah. «نطوى - بدأنا - نعیده»

6- He will recreate the folded-up creatures just like the initial creation. «نعیده»

7- Divine promises are certain and will be fulfilled. «انا كنا فاعلین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِى ٱلزَّبُورِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ٱلذِّكْرِ أَنَّ ٱلْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِىَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُونَ (۱٠٥)
Certainly We wrote in the Psalms, after the Torah: ‘Indeed My righteous servants shall inherit the earth.’ (105)

Key Points

The word Zabūr (زبور) in the Arabic language means any type of writing or book. «و كل شى ء فعلوه فى الزبر»(97) However, based on the context of the Ayah «و آتینا داود زبوراً»(98), it seems that the intent is the specific book of Prophet David (Dāwūd) عليه السلام, which contains a collection of his supplications, prayers, and admonitions. This book was revealed after the Torah (Tawrāt), and the intent of dhikr (ذِكْر) in this Ayah is also the Torah, just as it was mentioned in Ayah 48 of this same Surah that the Torah is dhikr. «و لقد آتینا موسى و هارون الفرقان و ضیاء و ذكراً للمتقین» Some have said that the purpose of Zabūr in this Ayah is all the heavenly books, and the intent of dhikr is the Glorious Qur'an. The word min ba‘d (من بعد) also comes to mean "in addition to," in which case the meaning of the Ayah becomes: We wrote in all the heavenly books, in addition to the Qur'an, that the inheritors of the earth will surely be My righteous servants. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about Zabūr and dhikr. He replied: Dhikr is with Allah, and Zabūr is the book revealed to David, and all the revealed books are with the People of Knowledge, and those are us (the Ahl al-Bayt).(99) In five instances in the Zabūr (Psalms) of Prophet David, which is today considered part of the Old Testament, similar expressions have appeared: that righteous men shall inherit the earth.(100) It is mentioned in numerous narrations that those righteous servants who will inherit the earth are the companions of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام.(101) In some narrations, we read that the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet of Islam علیهم السلام will inherit the earth, and the wicked people will be resurrected again.(102)

97) Qamar, 52. 98) Nisā’, 63 and Isrā’, 55. 99) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 225. 100) Tafsir Namūneh. 101) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 102) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 2, p. 297.

Messages

1- It is the Divine tradition that righteous servants should be the rulers and inheritors of the world. «كتبنا»

2- The prophecies of the Qur'an are certain. «لقد كتبنا فى الزبور ...»

3- The Qur'an informs about the future of history. «یرثها ...»

4- The future belongs to the righteous. «یرثها عبادى الصالحون»

5- Religion is not separate from politics. «یرثها عبادى الصالحون»

6- Worship alone is not sufficient; competence and righteous deeds are also necessary. «عبادى الصالحون»

7- Inheriting the earth and ruling the world require two conditions: servitude to Allah and performing righteous deeds. «عبادى الصالحون»

Ayah

إِنَّ فِى هَـٰذَا لَبَلَـٰغًا لِّقَوْمٍ عَـٰبِدِينَ (۱٠٦)
There is indeed in this a proclamation for a devout people. (106)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that the inheritance and governance of the righteous over the earth are realized through two conditions: first, sincere servitude to Allah, which was mentioned by the word «عبادى» (My servants), and second, acquiring competence, «الصالحون» (the righteous). In this Ayah, Allah emphasizes again that servants must receive this message: they must both become sincere in servitude, moving from the stage of general and common servitude «عابدین» to the stage of special servitude «عبادى», and they must strive to acquire competence. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «اللّه اللّه فِى القرآن، لا یَسبقنّكم فى العمل به غَیركم» I adjure you by Allah! I adjure you by Allah! Grasp the messages of the Qur'an, lest others surpass you in understanding its secrets, commands, and acting upon it!

Messages

1- The glad tidings and messages of the Qur'an are sufficient for guidance and felicity.(103) «لبلاغاً»

2- O servants of Allah, seek to acquire competence and merit so that you may inherit the earth. «یرثها عبادى الصالحون ان فى هذا لبلاغاً لقوم عابدین»

3- The good servants of Allah must receive the divine messages before anyone else. «لبلاغاً لقوم عابدین» (103) The word balāgh, according to Lisān al-'Arab, means sufficiency.

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠۷)
We did not send you but as a mercy to all the nations. (107)

Messages

1- The governance of righteous servants on Earth is the manifestation of grace and mercy achieved under the shadow of prophethood (Risalah). Everything that reaches individuals of the Prophet's mercy صلى الله علیه وآله in this world and the Hereafter is a reflection of Divine Mercy. «و ما ارسلناك الا رحمة»

2- All the rulings and commands brought by the Prophet of Islam, even Jihad, Hudud (prescribed punishments), Qisas (retaliation), and other penalties and penal laws, are a mercy for human society. «و ما ارسلناك الاّ رحمة»

3- Allah is «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the Worlds) and His Messenger is «رحمة للعالمین» (A Mercy to the Worlds). This means that true nurturing is possible under the guidance of the Prophets. «رحمة للعالمین»

4- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is a mercy for all human beings, in all ages, and in all places, and there is no need for another prophet. «رحمة للعالمین»

5- The noble Messenger of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is a mercy even for the close angels of Allah. «رحمة للعالمین» We read in a tradition (Hadith) that Gabriel told the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that your mercy included me as well.(104)

6- The commands of Islam are universal. «للعالمین»

7- A single human being, by Divine Will, can influence the entire existence. «للعالمین»(104) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّمَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۱٠۸)
Say, ‘It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So will you submit?’ (108)

Key Points

Imam Reza عليه السلام, amidst thousands of enthusiasts in Nishapur, in response to the people's request, narrated the sacred tradition (Hadith Qudsi) known as Silsilat al-Dhahab (The Golden Chain), stating that Monotheism (Tawhid) is the fortress and stronghold of Allah, and whoever enters it is secure in every respect. Then He said: The condition for this Monotheism is the acceptance of and obedience to the living Imam. «كلمة لااله الاّ اللّه حصنى فمن دخل حصنى امن من عذابى و انَا من شروطها»(105)

105) Bihar, Vol. 3, p. 7.

Messages

1- The invitation to Monotheism is a manifestation of Divine mercy. «رحمة للعالمین، قل انما یوحى »

Ayah

فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَقُلْ ءَاذَنتُكُمْ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٍ ۖ وَإِنْ أَدْرِىٓ أَقَرِيبٌ أَم بَعِيدٌ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ (۱٠۹)
But if they turn away, say, ‘I have proclaimed to you all alike, and I do not know whether what you have been promised is far or near. (109)

Key Points

| Question: Many verses and narrations indicate that the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله possessed extensive knowledge of the unseen (ilm al-ghayb), just as we read in Dua Nudbah: «و علمته علم ما كان و مایكون الى انقضاء خلقه» meaning, O God! You taught Your Prophet the knowledge of the past and the future of the world. However, in verses like the one above, we encounter phrases that indicate the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله lacked knowledge. Are these verses and narrations contradictory, or is there a way to reconcile them? Answer: Knowledge of the unseen is of two types. A portion of it is exclusive to the Divine Essence (Dhat Ahadiyyah), and no one has access to it, including the time of the occurrence of the Resurrection (Qiyamah), just as we read in the supplication: «و بحقّ علمك الّذى استأثرت به لنفسك» O God! [I ask You] by the right of Your knowledge which You have reserved exclusively for Yourself. However, the other portion of it is bestowed by Allah upon the Prophets, the Awliya (saints), and whomever He wills, as we see in this verse: «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیه الیك»(۱۰۶) just as many issues in the Quran consist of unseen matters. The divine promise for the disbelievers includes both defeat and destruction in wars and ruin, wrath, and punishment on the Day of Resurrection. «ما توعدون»

106) Aal-i-Imran, 44.

Messages

1- Some stubborn people turn away even from the "Mercy to the Worlds" (Rahmah li al-'Alamin). «و ما ارسلناك الاّ رحمة للعالمین... فاِن تولّوا»

2- Man is free (mukhtar), not compelled (majbur); thus, he can resist and turn away even from the invitation and request of the Prophets. «فاِن تولّوا»

3- One should not expect all people to accept the word of truth, as this goal was not achieved even for the Prophets. «فاِن تولّوا»

4- In the face of people's stubbornness and turning away, we must not lose our sense of responsibility. «فان تولّوا فقل»

5- All people are equally addressed by the warnings of the Prophets; gender, race, and language are not factors here. «آذنتكم على سواء» (Yes, in propagation, we must view all people equally) «على سواء»

6- Whatever we do not know, we should state clearly: I do not know. «اِن أدرى»

7- A certain set of knowledge is exclusive to the Holy Essence of Allah. «اِن أدرى أقریب»

Ayahs

إِنَّهُۥ يَعْلَمُ ٱلْجَهْرَ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْتُمُونَ (۱۱٠)
Indeed He knows whatever is spoken aloud, and He knows whatever you conceal. (110)
وَإِنْ أَدْرِى لَعَلَّهُۥ فِتْنَةٌ لَّكُمْ وَمَتَـٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۱۱۱)
I do not know—maybe it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a while.’ (111)

Key Points

If sometimes a delay is observed in the fulfillment of divine promises, this does not mean His unawareness or negligence, for He, according to the explicit text of the Quranic verse, knows everything and is aware of everything. Rather, it is due to various reasons, including: A: So that the transgressor can fill the measure of his sin to whatever extent he can. «انّما نُملى لهم لیزدادوا اِثما»(107) Indeed, We grant them respite so that their sins may increase. B: So that the wrongdoer may find success (divine grace) and repent. C: So that the good deeds of the sinner are consumed (in this world) and he no longer has any claim (reward) in the Hereafter.

107) Aal-i Imran, 178.

Messages

1- For Allah, the unseen and the manifest are no different. «یعلم الجهر ... یعلم ما تكتمون»

2- When Allah knows the hidden matters, why resort to conspiracy and hypocrisy? «یعلم ما تكتمون»

3- The knowledge of the Prophets is limited compared to the knowledge of Allah. «اِن أدرى»

4- The delay in divine wrath and punishment is either for the sake of trial (testing) or due to the filling of the measure of respite. «فتنة - متاع الى حین»

5- The successes and pleasures of this world are temporary. «الى حین»

Ayah

قَـٰلَ رَبِّ ٱحْكُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَرَبُّنَا ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ (۱۱۲)
He said, ‘My Lord! Judge [between us and the polytheists] with justice.’ ‘Our Lord is the All-beneficent; [He is our] resort against what you allege.’ (112)

Key Points

What is mentioned in the Quran regarding slanders against Allah is the people's imaginary description of Allah, not the true description. Therefore, we read: «و لكم الویل مما تصفون» (108) Woe to you for what you describe, or it is stated elsewhere: «سیجزیهم وصفهم» (109) Allah will soon punish them for their description, or in this verse He says: «المستعان على ما تصفون» I seek help from Allah against what you describe.

(108) Al-Anbiya, 18. (109) Al-An'am, 139.

Messages

  1. Sometimes obstinacy reaches a level that compels the Prophet, the "Mercy to the Worlds" («رحمة للعالمین»), to complain and invoke a curse. «رحمة للعالمین - قال ربّ احكم»

  2. In the face of the enemies' obstinacy, we should seek help from Allah. «واللّه المستعان»

  3. We should begin supplication (Du'a) with the word «ربّ» (Rabb/Lord). «ربّ احكم»

  4. Lordship (Rububiyyah) and judgment (Qada) have a close relationship. «ربّ احكم»

  5. The Absolute Knower who knows everything, His judgment is based on truth. «یعلم الجهر یعلم ما تكتمون - ربّ احكم بالحقّ»

  6. Even concerning enemies, we must not transgress the truth and should not ask for punishment beyond what is due. «ربّ احكم بالحقّ» (Of course, divine judgments and decrees are always based on truth, and it is we who must be satisfied with the measure of truth.)

  7. Even when invoking a curse or requesting punishment, we must not forget mercy, so that a window of hope remains open forever and for everyone. «ربّنا الرّحمن»

  8. Slanders and obscenities will not go unanswered. «المستعان على ما تصفون»

  9. Let us remember that accountability and reckoning are involved; therefore, we must be careful in our praises and criticisms. «المستعان على ما تصفون»
